recentReviews.comment: Dr. Hall is a challenging, caring, careful professor. He is very careful with his words. He also fairly and graciously represents views that oppose his own so that Students can understand where individuals who hold those beliefs are coming from. He made sure that each student got to know everyone else which helped us all participate.

Dr. Hall is truly a fantastic professor. I could go on and on about what makes him so amazing but I will just say this, if you ever get a chance to take him, do it. He will make you care more about what you are doing and absolutely fall in love with it more than any one else. He is extremely knowledgeable, helpful and genuinely one of the best.

BIBL 100 was my favorite class this semester, but also my hardest. I learned so much, Dr. Hall is super engaging and conversational in his classes and always invite students to come to office hours. He spent a lot of time explaining concepts to me and answering questions both on course material and on other questions I brought to him.

Such an amazing person AND professor. He really cares about what he teaches and who he teaches and really wants the students to learn. You have to put a lot of time into reading and understanding the material but it is so worth it because I have learned so much. I've walked out of that class with my mind blown from that day's lecture before.

Dr. Hall puts more effort into preparing lectures than any professor I know. He is dedicated to communicating truth with engaging content, peer-interactions, and meaningful coursework. His assignments take time and effort, and he is a tough grader, so don't do it for the grades alone--take his classes to grow & love Christ more. I promise you will!

(HUMA 303) My favorite HUMA professor by far. He makes everything very understandable and repeats himself a lot. In class, expect guided notes, fairly easy reading quizzes, an in-class essay with a pre-written outline, class discussions, and exams that are very possible to do well on if you study. Keep in mind that he is not "easy"; he is good.

Dr. McCray is a kind and brilliant professor. He has nearly a photographic memory and the best sense of British humor! He makes the material come alive and shows how much it matters to each of us. I love taking his classes because I know we will have a good time, learn a lot, and be cared for.

Had Dr. McCray for his first semester and can't wait to take him again. Even at an 8am class, he keeps everyone engaged. He is hilarious and kind, very smart, and gets students excited about important and complicated theological concepts. His accent is a plus :)

Dr. Moeller is an excellent professor. Her lectures are engaging and well-structured. She provides insightful feedback and encouragement. I loved taking Old Testament Scripture and Theology with her---I learned so much! She cares about her students and is intentional with her teaching methods and decisions with the class.

I tried to argue that my answer on a test should have higher points and she said if I keep talking she might lower it, shes mean and condescending and has unrealistic expectations and is the best teacher I ever had, if I didn't have to worry about my GPA i would take all her classes.

Dr. Moeller is my favorite professor so far. She is concise and clear in lectures and is not afraid to dive deep into hard topics. She is very personable and honest, which makes it easy to get to know her and ask questions. Although I had a good foundation in the Bible going into the class, I learned more than I expected and enjoyed each lecture.

She's extremely caring and will meet with you after hours. I didn't learn much in this class though, she teaches it too strangely. Uses phrases you'd never use yourself to describe basic things. But tests are a piece of cake as long as you memorize her wording.

Out of all the HUMA 102 professors, Dr. Moeller might be the best. You do have to do a lot of reading and some short writing assigments (and one longer paper), but its a pretty straightforward class. Tests can be a bit tricky because the formatting of tests changes slightly from test to test, but if you study well you'll do just fine.
He used to teach at penn state, actually avoid at all costs.

Took a lab with Dr. Beevers. Overall, just not a good teacher. I understand it was his first time teaching this class. But in comparison to the other labs section, his was 3-5x harder due to his tough grading. 
Several of the upperclassmen who had him in an actual class also said he is a tough grader and to avoid him if possible.

There were no lecture slides to follow, making note-taking and studying difficult. Lectures often felt disorganized and impractical, forcing me to teach most of the material to myself. On top of that, the quizzes and exams were unusually difficult, adding stress without clearly reinforcing the content.
It was a great class!
Sometimes lectures are good, but other times he is kind of confusing. But, he teaches directly from the Pearson textbook, so if you have ANY questions it is a super easy referral.
Professor Augspurger's lab sessions had only a few interesting science demonstrations but the rest was just in-class work that was boring to get through.

Augspurger was one of the worst teachers Ive had in a long time. His teaching style was so confusing, it seemed like he didnt care at all if we understood the material. He got frustrated when we all were averaging terribly on exams. His homeworks took forever to do as well.
Excellent professor. Difficult class but Dr. Bancroft teaches well and is very available to help during office hours. Committed to helping students understand the topics and succeed.
would not reccomend

Homework took around 16+ hours each week, as well as needing to do group projects and needin to study for his test every other week. Also the lectures were not helpful for homework at all. Would not recommend.
I did not enjoy this Discrete Math class at all and it's not easy, but, Bancroft did a great job helping everyone during office hours, especially for the proofs.

Nice but can be tough, willing to help.

Don't take if your a grumpy precalc student who doesn't want to show up to class like the other guy 😋

Dr. Stanton's Western Civ is a wild ride. His quizzes are insanely difficult and essay grading is tough.  Not approachable in office hours and not very understanding. Take anyone else for Western civ if you can.

Dr. Stanton is inconsistent. He gives pop quizzes that seem unrelated to course content. His paper is unreasonably difficult. However his exams are easy. He gives dry boring lectures and emphasizes things that you will never cover again. He quizzes/tests things on you that he just breezes over. There is a ton of reading, most of which is boring.

Pros:
1. Exams are very easy
2. Helpful in office hours
3. Don't need to read Pavloc; focus on lectures

Cons:
1. Very few grades: 5 quizzes (drops one), 2 exams, 1 mid-term paper, and participation
2. Quizzes are brutal
3. Midterm paper is a beast. Start early and go to office hours

Do not take Stanton for western civ. He is rude, lectures are dry and very opinionated, and everything you are graded on is unfairly hard. Pop quizzes, midterm and final, and paper with about 48 rules you must follow. He is super grumpy and not helpful. There is also a lot of reading, but to be expected for every HUMA. Avoid him if possible.

I would not recommend Dr. Stanton for HUMA 200 Western Civ. It's hard to tell from his lectures what is important material that will be on the test and what is just elaboration. He grades by very few tests, so if you do poorly on one, you do poorly in the class. However, he is very accessible and helpful in office hours.
Dr Clauss lectures can be quite long and hard to follow but for the most part she teaches the topics very well, and is very easy to talk to if you need extra help.

Dr. Clauss is rough but she's probably your only option. Similar to last semester, expect a ton of homework where only 1 is graded. I felt that the content in this course was way harder than Mechanics I. Plus Dr. Clauss moves at light speed through lectures, is condescending/not super helpful in office hours, and is an extremely hard grader.

It's a hard class, but she's an awesome professor. Gives out practice exams before the real ones, and if you can do the homework well, you'll do well on the tests cause they're super similar. Works to accommodate student schedules as best she can.

Oh Mama Clauss...can't avoid her, but she is a really great professor. Mechanics is difficult, so make sure you use her office hours because she will do everything to help you. She understands the needs of her students and will adjust test dates to better fit everyone's schedule. Goes very fast in class, so pay attention and take notes!
Smokin. Hard test though. Good in office hours.
Dr Li is very straight forward, even if you don't pay attention as long as you have the notes and do the hw you will do fine on the quizzes and test.

Dr. Li cares a lot about her student, and understands their situations. The issue is she's overloaded with freshmen classes and often fails to keep a good class space. It's hard to tell who's at fault, her or the college, but the quality of her lectures is poor. Be neat and legible on her tests and you'll do better.

Easily the most disorganized experience I've ever had to take part of. Very inconsistant and harsh grading on exams. Peers of mine were getting wildly more or less points taken off for the same mistakes. She isn't terrible at explaining new material. If someone asked a question that required further explaining, you may as well pack up and leave.

Dr. Li is a very kind woman. However, she is not a very good teacher. She herself doesn't understand the information she is trying to teach us, doesn't hold up to her promises, and spends more time on things we are not tested on than things we are. She was only hired because there were no other candidates.

Mrs. Gwynn is very nice but definitely a new teacher. Science is hard to teach, so give her some slack. She is very helpful and sweet. Class is a lot of info at once. You do not need the textbook. Tests are worth a lot.
He is an icon- so funny, caring, wise, and sweet. Worth taking even if not required. You will learn so much and will not regret it

Cara is amazing! She is so chill and makes class so fun, even when the topics are heavy. No tests, no final, just some projects which are honestly creative and very enjoyable. Class is more like a discussion and she is the sweetest person ever. Highly recommend!

His lectures had nothing to do with the dozens of quizzes you have throughout the semester -- at least two a week. He couldn't figure out the answers to his own problems. Do not take any of his classes.

Most papers and projects are graded on a 1-4 scale, with 3/4 being you did everything correctly, making a 4/4 basically impossible. He is very intelligent but doesn't bring it down to a level others can understand. There are lots of proofs and equations that are talked about once in class for 3 minutes, then on the exams with multiple questions.

Extremely hard exams but gives lots of partial credit for correct methods/work. Has trouble bringing calculus down to the level of a non math/engineering major, but if you follow along in class and do the homework you'll be fine. funny guy, gives lots of opportunity for collaboration/group work sessions.

If you are thinking about taking this class run the other way. He does not help at all during office hours and is very derogatory. His test are not like his homework or quizzes, they are much worse. And just taking this class alone takes a solid 3-4+ hours a day.
Don't take this professors classes

After two years, this was the first class I didn't get an A in. I regularly questioned what level he thought this class was for, as I've been a history major long enough. I felt like I was taking a 500 class. His attitude was pretentious and pedantic. He acted like nothing impressed him. Avoid any class with his name on it.

Really enjoyed Dr. Clemms Western civ. Have to stay up to date on reading to be prepared for quizzes - pay attention to pictures/captions in the text as many questions come from those for quizzes. Class attendance and participation are important - ask a question or comment once a class for an easy 15% in your grade. Fair grading. Funny & engaging.
Very difficult, does not want to help you out at all if you go to his office hours. Toughest grader on papers and tests. I would not suggest this professor to anyone.

You do need to work to get a good grade in his classes, but grading is fair. Interesting lectures, thought-provoking discussions, and has a great sense of humor. Always would take his classes if given the choice.

Loved his lectures, always made them interesting. But he is a tough grader, you have to work for it. Essay questions have to answer everything exactly right if you want full credit. But, if I was a history major, I would take him again.

Dr. Mitchell is incredibly knowledgeable about what he teaches, and his lectures are very engaging and interesting. His grading scale, however, seems more strict than is appropriate for a class of all freshmen. The amount of work in his Western Civ class was significantly higher than that of other sections. Hard to get a hold of in office hours.

Dr. Mitchell is the most frustrating professor I have ever had. You many only see 2 grades all year, and the midterm and final will expect you to remember 1000 pages of reading. In the last half of the semester, he failed to have open office hours for 4 weeks (or even show up) and refused to answer questions over email. I could never recommend him.

The hardest part of the class was not collapsing on the floor from laughter. His rhetorical abilities: unmatched. His teaching style: lucid and intuitive. The board is his chaotic masterpiece. He challenges not only the mind but the heart, both the intellect and the will. His assesments are righteous. He expects a lot but gives more.

Dr. Mitchell is engaging and tough. You will have to work hard for anything above a C. That being said, he is an amazing lecturer and a super passionate guy. I learned a lot in his class, and, though he can be a little disorganized, you will grow so much as a student and person.

Mitchell seems to know about everything that ever happened.  He uses lots of quotes in his lectures which makes them really interesting to go over a second time. He can be a bit all over the place sometimes, but he clearly is very passionate about his work. He's very approachable, and can be very forgiving on assignments, as long as you're trying.

Dr. Paz is the goat; she is super caring and is very effective at teaching material. She was easy to get hold of outside of class and was very helpful. She involved students in interactive examples to help them grasp the material. The difficult of the class was entirely a result of the nature of the material and not a result of Dr. Paz's teaching.

Taking a Biology course with Dr. Paz is one of the best things you could do with your time. She's a brilliant professor, excellent lecturer, and even kinder of a person. She clearly loves to teach and she's good at it. Science can be an intimidating field, but she explains even the most difficult of concepts in a clear and concise manner. Love her!

Dr. Pazehoski made BIOL 101 an enjoyable class.  She is really kind and helpful and is great about answering questions.  Dr. Pazehoski explains the course content really well; the content can be challenging, but if you study (use the practice exams) and have a good understanding of the content, you should be good to go.  Highly recommend!

Dr. Paz was an amazing professor, especially for an incoming freshman still getting settled into college. If you show up to lecture and take notes on her PowerPoints, you should have no problem. She gives out practice exams before the tests too, which help a lot in telling you what you do and do not know. Great professor and would highly recommend.

She's just the sweetest ever. She really cares for her students. Cell bio is already difficult but she makes it way more bearable. She records her lectures online and posts them. The actual material is actually insanely hard.

Dr. West is a very thoughtful and wise teacher who I think is seldom appreciated enough by grovers. While his grading can be harsh at times, an A is achievable if you follow his guidelines and listen to his comments closely. His teaching style is quite laid back and restrained, and his class often felt more like a seminar than an actual class.

I took HUMA100 with Dr. West, and while the class wasn't insanely difficult (there are no exams), his grading on the 300 word reflections was quite harsh. He would be insanely critical of small syntax irregularities in a class on Doctrine, and made it clear he didn't think many students should ever get A letter grades. Passable, but not the best.

Dr. West is the most aggravating professor I've EVER met on this campus.
He made us memorize 17 verses for a 2% QUIZ.
He relies on his readings which are decent, but his teaching is DRY. MONOTONE. BORING. These reviews are true, don't listen to the grovers who swear "Take it for the grove city experience." You want this experience? No you DON'T

Dr. West has to be one of the worst professors on this campus. Being a freshman, I had no other option for my HUMA 100 class. Please head warning and avoid him at all costs. He is boring, unengaging, and dry. He made us sit in a graveyard and reflect on our funerals for extra credit. Everything he did was so weird and I always left angry. AVOID

Please listen to the negative reviews written about this professor. They are not exaggerating or lying in the slightest. Class is treated like mass, you have to MEMORIZE a 17 verse Bible passage for a grade, and at some point he does ask for you to think about your funeral......and it's graded. Find another proffessor if you have the option!

Dr. Foster is sweet and knowledgeable about chemistry, but often seems to get overwhelmed in class and sometimes makes mistakes. Many of his exams had errors in them that he would correct mid-exam. There was a fair amount of extra credit offered, which allowed me to get a high A. He gets the job done for gen chem.

It's a hard class, teaching isn't very organized, and you have to teach yourself outside of class to succeed. If you don't put in the effort, you won't get what you want, but that's life. He has lots of grace for students, however, and he will allow points back in labs and has some exam grade curves.

He runs through lectures, and the exams are so difficult. He's really dragging it with the impossible quizzes every Friday. He never gives enough time on the quizzes and exams. Just doesn't teach well. Tutors are way more helpful. I should've skipped class to save time and just taught myself and got help from tutors.

Very caring and enthusiastic teacher, always willing to help out even if he is not in office hours. Boy are his exams hard, but he adds points/curves them to benefit the class. Even gives opportunities to earn back exam points and gives tons of bonus opportunities. you will learn very much from Dr Foster, I highly recommend taking his chem with him

This professor has little to no office hours, is very disorganized and doesn't follow lesson plans or chapters, he gives no time for quizzes and exams no partial credit for showing work or trying, just a pass or fail grade. Terrible lectures no visuals, not good teacher
She treated everyone like little kids and got kind of annoying. The class is not bad but every assignment feels kind of pointless and I feel like I didn't learn anything.
She was very nice and helpful.

I have her at Slippery Rock and I'm very surprised by the bad ratings. She is honestly a sweetheart and even made me a personalized gift at the end of her classes because I switched majors. She is very helpful and cares enough to remember everyone's names. It is an easy A as long as you show up and work with your groups.

I had Dr.Fecich for a block class of mine (which is not listed above as an option). I have mixed feelings towards her teaching, she was very kind to me but when it came to turning things in, I felt as if she did not understand what she was doing and she wasn't clear. Had a great experience with her aside the minor things. I would suggest her again!

My experience in this course has been beyond disappointing. I have found the professor's approach to be less than supportive, and I struggle to understand why a professor would be unwilling to assist students in their academic growth. There appears to be a noticeable bias in how students are treated. She is altogether not kind and not helpful.

Dr. Brower is amazing- so caring, funny, accessible, a great teacher, makes class structure easy to follow along and do well. He is so smart and passionate, and really cares about his students DUDE! Has high expectations but explains things and answers questions so well.
Says dude like 20 times a class. Funny, good teacher, Dr. Brower is awesome dude.

DUDE! Take Dr. Brower, like literally right now, go sign up. He is sooo funny and has jokes and quirks for days. You could answer a question so astronomically wrong and he'll just be like "Dudeeeee love where your head is at! Not quite right but let's talk about it". Big Crush from Nemo energy.

If you're not taking a class with Dr.Brower, you're making the wrong decision.  He has amazing lectures, cares deeply about his students, and knows how to teach.  Also, DUDE!

Be prepared to work really hard in this class.  Its totally worth it though.
Great guy, engaging. By far he takes the cake for best professor of all time. Dude...

Dr. Loretto is super funny and explains things in a very interesting way. You will have to read a ton, as with any civ lit class, but assignments are straightforward. Participation does matter, so make sure you are engaged. He does tend to make it hard to get an a, but overall great guy and enjoyable class!

Dr. Loretto was a very good professor when I took him for Writing 101. There are three papers and a final essay. I do not enjoy writing, but he gave great tips to become a better writer, as well as gave us opportunities to work together for peer review, and the final essay is partially worked on in a group. Overall, definitely should take his class

Dr. Loretto is so fun! I hate writing a lot but he makes class interesting. You will do a lot of group work during class but most of your papers are done individually (minus peer reviews). He does assign reading every day but I very rarely did it. I would still get the books though because there will be random times when you will need it.

Dr. Loretto is the best CIV LIT professor. His genuine care for his students' success is evident in his teaching approach. While the CIV LIT class entails a significant amount of reading and reading quizzes, these quizzes are easy. This relaxed atmosphere makes the class enjoyable and conducive to learning. I recommend taking Dr. Loretto.

Loretto is a fairly nice and decent prof- albeit a touch dry and the class itself is somewhat dull. However, it was a fairly easy class to get an A in, and I absolutely loved the group I was assigned to work with throughout the semester. Not so bad for a required class. (Just beware the Little Seagull online exercises.)

Dr. Hosack is very sweet and the class is chill and laid back. Pretty unstructured though, which can be overwhelming at times. Grading criteria is often not clear and test questions are confusing, making it hard to do well. Participation and discussion matters, so be engaged. Light homework. Interesting course overall!

you do a lot of different things in this class, its a frankenstine of different teaching subjects and styles, also it feels like the course was made in 2014 and never updated, not an east A type class but I wasn't trying

If you want to succeed in this class, you must read the textbook. The lectures are very helpful and Dr. Coulter is genuinely a kind, caring professor. The best strategy to study for the tests is to read all of the textbook chapters that are assigned, watch the lectures, and make a study sheet for both. This class is a great use of your time!

Dr. Coulter is a funny, kind, clear, caring professor. I highly recommend getting the textbooks for this class and reading every single word, as all of the tests and assignments are heavily based on it. The lectures will be your best friend as they help summarise the heavy textbook reading. The things taught in this class will equip you for life.

Dr. Coulter seemed nice, but the assignments were confusing and given at random times. Lacked organization. Be prepared to wait almost a month to receive any papers back. The lectures were long and he got distracted very easily. Only a few assignments accounted for most of your grade.

Dr. Coulter is very kind. He cares about knowing each of his students. If you seek him out in office hours, he will do whatever he can to help you out. I really appreciate how knowledgeable he is. He is bit boring as a lecturer, but if you listen and care, you will certainly learn.
great professor, the content he teaches is easy to learn and mostly conceptual, so if you don't care you wont suffer but will lose out on valuable wisdom
He is a great lecturer, has lots of group discussions, and many interactive activities. Would definitely recommend taking Sweet for a class.

YOO great prof. Professor sweet has engaging lectures, does everything in his power to make his students enjoy his class, and is very forgiving and understanding. His exams can be tough if you don't pay attention or read but if you do those things, nice east A or B.

The class was fine but Prof. Sweet is the best. He is the single connection and reason I got several jobs/opportunities. He cares about students and will help you succeed. 

Pro tip: the Center for Entrepreneurship + Innovation is amazing for students of any major, and you should check their opportunities out.

I had an amazing experience with Professor Sweet! He took the time to explain things clearly and made every class engaging and never boring. He was always willing to help, no matter what I needed assistance with. Highly recommend!
likes to use teams, teaching is his second job to balance tho and it shows, so use teams to contact,

His teaching style is UNFATHOMABLY dull. Im a senior comm major so Ive taken a lot of courses in the creative realm. I even took a design class in HS & it was super fun/hands-on, but Mucha gives monotone lectures the whole time. I didnt know it was possible to make design boring. Its an easy A class, but bring a book/other homework to pass the time
Professor Mucha helped me develop my portfolio website and gave me good insight for improvement. I wish we spent more of the semester doing that as opposed to the other projects.

Professor Mucha is a fantastic professor who gives good feedback on your design projects. He is available online if you have questions though it may take some time before he gets back to you. Overall, I recommend him to anyone taking DESI 201.

I have really enjoyed taking classes with Nate! He's a chill guy and he is always willing to help you in any way he can. All his communication is through Slack and he answers back pretty quickly. Design can be confusing and vague at first, but once you understand the basics, you can enjoy it a lot more. Take his classes!

He was a kind professor. Missed classes on multiple occasions though and many of the quizzes (which were the only things graded other than essays) were difficult and felt as though they did not relate to truly improving our own writing. Essays were often graded barely before the next one was due as well, so I was unable to fix mistakes on the next.

Avoid taking him as a professor if you can. He is a kind man, but not great professor
-The only things that are graded in the class are the essays and quizzes
-Mostly reading articles and discussing them (and difficult pop quizzes)
-Takes a while to grade, and bad at giving constructive feedback
-Doesn't really teach how to improve your writing

As an ed major, it is hard to sit in a class where everything we are taught about being a teacher is thrown out the window. Don't get me wrong he is polite and kind but his class is hard because it is so difficult to know where you are actually succeeding.

- Quizzes weigh more than essays
- When given essays back he doesn't give critical feedback, and it took him 6 weeks to grade which doesn't allow us to do better as a student
- His attendance was awful, missing classes based on minor situations missed 7/28.
- HIs syllabus was inaccurate most of the time (changed his syllabus about 3 times)

He is a very kind professor however he changed numerous times and still did not hold to the dates that he promised to have our papers by. We had to turn another paper without the feedback from the previous one.
Interesting class and very caring professor.
I really liked him! He is super helpful and a super interesting guy to get to know outside of class.
Super nice. Wants to help you succeed. A lot of homework but that is standard for a math class.
He is constantly striving to improve and learn from his students as he teaches them. He is open and honest about mistakes and very humble.

Dr. Drai overall does a great job with teaching business calc. Most people in this class are in it simply because it is required for their business major. He is always open to questions and will explain things as many times as needed to get you to understand a concept thoroughly. His tests are difficult, and his final is very hard.

Dr. Conway is awesome! Super engaging, funny, and class is organized to help students succeed. Content will challenge you, but Conway explains well, is super helpful, and a very fair grader. There are opportunities for extra points, and while material is complex, hard workers will do well and appreciate his grading structure.

His lectures are engaging and easy to follow and take notes on, he uses interesting examples, and he is just such a nice, cool, caring guy. He gives study guides for each exam and curves well. Fair grader, accessible, knowledgeable, super funny. I feel like I have learned so much from him. Can't recommend this class enough!

Conway is the best teacher at GCC. He actually cares about students and wants them to succeed. He writes everything you need to know on the board. The only time the book is needed is for the tests. There's in class activities that boost your grade. This class is an easy class that is actually interesting and useful.
Like so many others, he's kind of quirky. But it makes for a fun class. I'd say he's the best rounded prof in the psyc department as far as engaging, informative, and helpful.
Sensation and Perception with Dr Conway was a great class. It wasn't difficult but you had to read the textbook for exams. He is a really fair grader.

As a STEM major, I really enjoyed having Dr. Shepherd for WRIT 101-- she gives good feedback on your writing and the essay prompts for her class are actually quite interesting! I also learned some great writing tips from her class that I'm never going to forget. Also, her classes were more discussion-based than lecture-based, which I liked.

Pretty chill for Civ lit, on average the readings were ok, sometimes were a bit much and quite confusing but such is life. Helpful in office hours but will respond to your email about a quiz today in 2 weeks.

I didn't mind Dr Sheperd. However, her class wasn't super enjoyable. She's kinda monotone but other than that, she's nice and genuinely wants to help you become a better writer. We only had 3 essays plus our final paper, which isn't horrible. She lets you basically pick your own topic, as long as it follows the style of essay which is great.

It's Writing 101. Nobody likes Writing 101. Take Shepherd and you'll pass. Easy enough. Ima be honest it's really hard to tell because WRIT101 no matter who you take is gonna be boring. Just write the papers and you'll pass. Don't be lazy. No final, just a final paper

Anderson can be a bit grating as a professor, but is really intelligent. Some of his claims about evolution seem dismissive (despite berating others in the field for the same thing) and you need to just deal with his occasional rants, but you do learn quite a bit. You MUST keep up with the reading, and the midterm is very hard.

If you want an idea of who Dr. Anderson is, his favorite phrase is “If you're feeling unsure about *claim*, don't worry because NOBODY believes that.” Believes anyone without a PhD is unintelligent. I thought this class would be informative, but he just repeats the same lecture every day about how people are having fake conversations about science.

Yeah so Dr. Anderson is not a great professor. You could probably get that from every other post too. Pop quizzes, tons of reading, the most boring lectures, hard tests. If you don't do work, do not take this class. I got an A, and I am telling you to steer away. I am so genuinely surprised he still has a job here. Take this as a hint: quit.

SSFT with Dr. Anderson was a fairly easy class, but it was VERY boring. If you do the readings the day before class, you'll do fine on the quizzes. The midterm and final were pretty easy as long as you studied the study guide. The project takes time but he isn't a very hard grader. Just do the work and study and you'll do fine.

his group project is super annoying but it will boost your grade. lectures are so boring and he always talks about the same thing, i didn't really learn that much tbh. his midterm was awful. overall was an ok class
Very difficult.
Challenging class.

Dr Flanders is awesome. He is really funny and gets very passionate about math concepts. He can be pretty specific in his grading so it takes time to get used to what all he is looking for in terms of steps and potential for partial credit. Overall, I have learned so much and he is so awesome.
One of the better educators on the face of our planet.
Had him for stats. Seems boring at first but is lowkey funny. Homework and quizzes can be a bit tough but it's a really clearly laid-out course.

Dr. Fuller is a fairly clear professor, and this class is not difficult to pass. The extra credit is not very hard and can add 4% to the final grade. He can feel a little condescending, especially if you ask questions, and getting into the few office hours he has is difficult, as he typically came 30 minutes late. Take notes and pay attention.

By far the most understanding professor I have had. He has s very nice and approachable. He really allows for creativity in the classroom with open class discussions and multiple extra credit assignments. He also has a great sense of humor and isn't afraid to include jokes into his lessons. I gained a good education and he was my favorite professor
Most used phrases: "Do you think there's going to be a quiz today?" and "...which brings us to today's topic."

One of the best profs on campus. Engaging classes, fun (will play you in tennis), and down to earth. The tests are tricky. Go slow and read the questions carefully. Need to study a week out, but don't let that scare you from taking it. Great class
Best teacher I have ever had but Intermediate Micro is tough

I was very skeptical of philosophy even as this class started, and really struggled at first. However, Dr. Franklin's passion and willingness to ensure his students' understanding helped me come to appreciate the class and really understand the material. Keep up with your Commonplacing and do all the reflections and you will do well!

This class is nonsense. There's nothing more to say about it, it's a waste of time and if you don't know what logic is, well let me tell you, it's so hard to comprehend. He literally doesn't help you if you go to office hours, "you need to practice more" 
Okay Christopher, if a is q, and p is valid this class is 
INVALID.

His lectures are relatively interesting and he is obviously passionate about the subject. I had an ok experience, but I wouldn't choose to take this professor again and wouldn't recommend him due to lack of professionalism. The opinion of him on campus is very divided and people are generally either big fans or avoid his classes entirely.

Dr. Franklin taught me so much in intro to philosophy. The class required effort but was not too difficult. The lectures were engaging and the readings were interesting. He randomly calls on people in class, but is kind enough to not be intimidating. Will definitely take one of his classes again!
By far the best professor I have taken so far. Just do your homework and your grade will be fine. Expect fascinating and interactive lectures.

I have loved taking French with Dr. Madsen. She makes language very low-stress and always encourages her students to push the boundaries of their French. Quizzes are the hardest part of the class, but even if you struggle there, the tests are totally passable if you have taken the comprehensive practice tests. I would recommend her to any student!

Dr. Madsen is literally incredible. She is super helpful in class, easy to talk to outside of class, easygoing in office hours, and fairly organized. She does tend to focus more on her higher level French classes rather than her 101-202 classes, so sometimes grading can come back late, but that really doesn't matter in the long run. 10/10 take her.

Dr. Madsen is energetic, personable, well organized, and even students who dislike French seem to truly enjoy engaging with her in class. She has lots of experience, explains all material clearly and at a good pace, and has a true passion for the Francophone culture, history, and her students. Would highly recommend her for any class.

Dr. Madsen has only been teaching at GCC for a couple years but has already made an incredible impression. She goes above and beyond in everything she does to ensure that her students understand the material and enjoy it. She knows the workload students have and so is very understanding and helpful with assignments. She is very intellegent and fun.

She is a very good professor. She teaches a lot about culture, such a phrases, dos and don'ts, and food. She is extremely kind and wants to make sure you learn and she'll do what she can to help. She is very pleasant to talk to and nonthreatening in her office hours. She is the one who convinced me to get a french minor.

If you are considering taking a music course or playing in orchestra, DO IT!! Tedford is a joy to work under, and celebrates friendships within the ensemble. The schedule is less than it seems, and the caliber of musicality is excellent. I couldn't imagine not being in orchestra at GCC.

Dr. Tedford is an outwardly sarcastic and blunt individual who deeply cares about his students and has a vast knowledge of music. Anyone who has taken a class of his knows he wants his students to succeed.
Best thing the Grove City Music Department could have ever done! Good Bless JT!
God's gift to the music department. Tedford is an amazing man and a great conductor.
Funny director and tries not to be too serious but when things get tough, he starts cracking down a bit.
Good person, hard prof

Professor Herbener did speak in a monotone voice but I am not understanding the poor reviews. He does a great job at explaining economic concepts and gives useful examples for different topics. Also didn't grade as harsh as some of the other professors at GCC. Out of all the Econ professors I have had, I learned the most from Herbener.

Never in all my years have I felt so disrespected by a teacher. Every time a student asked a question, he made them feel dumb. Direct quotes from his slides didn't get full credit on quizzes.
Here are some notable quotes from him:
"why are you looking puzzled at this?"
"are you even a business major?"
"look it up and educate yourself on this"

Dr Herbener is completely out of touch with his students, and not in a good way.  His lectures are very boring and he shows little effort to change that. His language is confusing and unnecessary, leading to confusion. When you ask questions he gets mad! Overall, he doesn't give much effort into making this class better. Don't recommend

Prepare to work hard in this challenging Econ course. Professor Herbener's lectures can be dry, but engaging with the material and using helpful study tools like unstuck AI can really pay off. Stay on top of the readings and practice those tricky multiple-choice exams.

Definitely the best Western Civ professor available. Each lecture is fascinating and he is passionate about the subject. Not always in line which mainstream narratives, which makes the class even more interesting. Will encourage you to think outside of your cultural presuppositions.
Amazing lectures that make you rethink a lot of presuppositions of American society. He is a very fair grader and is clear with expectations in the class.

I'm a CS major but and yet most of my education from college was shaped by Dr. Edwards. I was a terrible reader, and even worse writer, but after taking this class I have a strong passion for both and was inspired to be a better man. NOT the class to take for easy grade, but if you want to be a better man or woman, I would recommend it.

The best professor I've had (I took Found Ed & Western Civ). He's changed the way I see the world! You'll be reading a ton but it's worth it - don't be afraid to do hard things! College isn't just about getting a grade, it's about developing your mind & soul with humility, curiosity, and diligence. Dr. Edwards truly helps you achieve this.

For a mandatory huma class, the level of difficulty is insane. He gives pop quizzes but assigns about fifty pages of reading every class. His tests are impossible. He asks fifty fill-in-the-blank questions and spelling counts. I ended up withdrawing because I know my limits as a human being. His lectures are okay but he makes everything else awful

Dr. Hubbard made Greek so fun! he's super funny and accessible outside class, I thoroughly enjoyed my time in class and in office hours. He made the language easy to grasp yet also allowed the course to challenge learners. Participation definitely matters in class, he will cold call on students to answer questions, and often has group learning.

Dr. Ansberry is super funny and engaging in lectures. He has a great balance between lecture and discussion in class and really finds ways to let students answer their own questions. Take good notes in lectures as most of the material you'll be tested on for the final will come directly from lectures.

Dr. Carruth is one of the best professors I have ever had. She allows time for so much practice with Spanish during class it makes learning everything very easy. She is also a very genuinely good person to talk to!

The class was disappointing because Ms. Carruth failed to create an immersion environment. For example, a student asked her what "conseguir" meant, and rather than using circumlocution to EXPLAIN it in Spanish by likening it to "obtener" and "tener," she took the easy way out and just said that it means "to get."
Not an easy class, but I appreciate the useful guidance and practice in the language!
Dr. Carruth is very energetic, helpful, and effective in her teaching style.
I love Dr. Carruth's lectures. She's super passionate and it definitely carries over into her teaching. Her exams and quizzes are fairly easy as well as long as you make sure to study.

Dr. Kosits is a great teacher who is both passionate and knowledgeable about his field. Pay attention to the lectures and do the reading along with all the little practice tests on Mindtap and you will be good to go. He is also genuinely hilarious, so if you are actually invested in what he has to say the class period will be very enjoyable.

Dr. Kosits is an amazing professor. He is pretty difficult though. It is important to pay attention in his classes. If you do so, you will most likely do fine! I have had him for multiple classes, and I have never got less than a B. He welcomes many questions, and he is very passionate about what he teaches!

Dr. Horton's expertise is overshadowed by his unprofessional and hostile behavior. He publicly humiliated me (in front of the class), calling me “disappointing,” and reacted with anger over a legitimate, pre-notified absence. Such disrespect makes the classroom intimidating rather than conducive to learning. This CLASS was utterly disappointing!

Dr. Horton is knowledgeable, but his classroom conduct was often frustrating. He publicly called me “disappointing,” which felt disrespectful and unprofessional. Despite advance notice for an excused absence (over 3 hours prior), he reacted with visible anger. Greater professionalism and respect would improve the learning environment. Take caution!

Super weird prof, very out of pocket. Best laugh ever and tells weird stories. Not warm and fuzzy, calls himself a benevolent dictator and cell phone n@zi. Lecture heavy, moves very fast, and lots of reading. If you know the stuff, you'll do well. Extensive group paper project and he is a harsh grader, so put the work in. Overall just odd.

Some say he's not great, but his class and the way he taught it was my favorite. He is a little quirky, but it's fun and engaging. You'll never be bored in class, especially if you're interested in children, education, or psychology. The tests are multiple choice and do require reading on your own, but he gives a lot of info in class. He's so fun!

He was very good at teaching and lectured well. He does go a little fast and I fell behind on notes. It's definitely a lot of reading so make sure to keep up with that. But overall he is very good at what he does and explains things in a fun and interesting way.

Hands down, Dr. Bright is the best professor I have ever had at GCC. I took him four times, and I wish I could have taken him more. I took every psychology elective that I could with him. I have NEVER had one bad experience with him. Pay attention, laugh, smile, have fun. You WILL pass. You WILL have fun. You WILL love Dr. Bright!!

Dr. Bright is a phenomenal professor. I had a 2 hour and a half night class with him and he made it feel like an hour-long class. Dr. Bright is engaging and frequently makes goofy jokes that help keep your attention in class. His courses are extremely easy and he makes clear what will be on the exam. If you have the option, take Dr. Bright.

(psyc 312) He actually wants us to learn and is very engaging. Any class with him will be the best class you take. He's always telling jokes and stories. Tests are super easy and clear. Genuinely one of the best professors I've taken here.

Dr. Bright is the best professor ever- so kind, caring, funny, and entertaining. Reading is not bad at all, very little work, and his class is so enjoyable you will be looking forward to. He is very relational and easy to reach. You will learn a lot but have fun while doing it. Seriously cannot recommend taking a Bright class enough.
I loved Dr. Bright! I took this class as a freshmen and it was so insightful. He is SO FUNNY and really gets to know the students. 100% take this professor for any class.
Dr. Dreves' Educational Psychology class was definitely my favorite class this semester! She is so engaging during class and clearly wants every student to succeed.
I LOVE DR. DREVES. She is simply the best and so so willing to help at all times. Big fan of her.

Dr. Dreves is an amazing professor! Everything that she teaches about regarding education she models in her own teaching. Her notes make the concepts easy to understand but studying is necessary because of all the similar acronyms on the tests. She is so caring and patient and is happy to help you if you ever need it.
This professor is supper caring and makes sure to keep the class interesting. She gives good feedback and is easy to talk to if you would have any questions or concerns.

Dr. Dreves is truly passionate about what she teaches! She gives you guided notes to follow along with which may seem silly, but quizzes/tests are taken right from those! There is a field with this class that is a big chunk of your grade so if you are not the best test taker, this helps! You will LOVE Dr. Dreves!

Had him for wisdom literature—amazing lecturer, provides really helpful outlines, and very manageable workload. He's a very fair grader, so if you do the work you'll do well. There were also no exams in this class—even the final was take-home and consisted of essay questions, so that's a plus.
Great elective to take, informative and interesting. A good, enjoyable low maintenance class that would be good to take if you have a hard semester.

Dr. Richter was pretty clear with his expectations. I wish I could've done better on the exams, but I didn't do horrible. He just asked a couple of random questions. He gives study guides, and he usually gives 100% for discussions, and such. Overall, pretty kind and clear professor!

Too many assignments for a 100-level course. Don't write too well or she'll accuse you of using AI, give you a 0 the night before study day, & won't contact you. When you send her proof you didn't, she backtracks & denies she thought you did. She also missed our presentations & refused to fix a grade after agreeing it was discrepant. Avoid her.
Material is easy, class is easy, but value of class is not worth it...? Lots of videos rather than lectures, but she is caring and a fair grader.

For a conservative Christian college community, Dr. Hollenberger is not as asset. Although she says she is offering multiple perspectives, lectures and readings are pretty one-sided (liberal). Her applications of Scripture are often misleading and wrong. These classes make me regret taking up social work. However, she is genuine and kind.

Hollenberger is a little boring, but her class is pretty easy. Be careful with her study guides because they don't have everything you need to know by a long shot. I did terrible on the midterm because of this. Other than that her papers are super easy and she doesn't grade them harshly, which is nice because they take up a big chunk of your grade.

She was nice and considerate of students and our time. The content was kind of self-explanatory, but she delivered the material in many ways so class was engaging. Also included lots of group discussions. The papers were long and time consuming, but she was a very fair grader and the midterm and final were easy and only on lecture material.

To be completely honest, Bandy is an amazing professor. Some things he does is weird, like at first he seems very harsh with grading, attendance and stuff like that, but realistically it's all fine. Class is not hard, just actually do your work, it's easy. Final is long tho. Have to take a ton of in class notes, he does not post the slides either.
Professor Bandy cares very much about filmmaking and will tell you what you need to here so you can improve. It's mostly team projects for this course so make sure you put in your share of the work.

I've taken Prof B three times now- and as redundant as he is on his past experience and work within the video production world, I still find ways to respect him more and more. If you are not familiar with how to edit videos and use Premiere, get ready for a learning curve and time after class to get the hang of it.

Definitely makes you work for an A. Relies on his lab assistants to teach the nitty-gritty parts of editing--he only does the "big" stuff and doesn't understand how the programs work. Does know the theory behind the craft well. Comes off as bluffing and arrogant, but it's just his style. He is easily impressed with bells and whistles on tech

I took Dr. Bandy for Journalism and enjoyed his class immensely. He is intimidating on day one, but once getting to know him you'll learn he has a big heart. He gives news article assignments and forum posts that aren't hard if you work for it. Bandy is a tough grader, but he pushes you to be a better writer and student. I would take him again!

Dr. Ault is a great professor. Take this class if you need an easy two credits. Yes you'll have to study for the mid-term and final. The homework assigned are easy. Two homework assigned combined are also more than the mid term is worth. Just do what is assigned and you'll come out of it with an A

She's so sweet and her class is interesting. This is probably one of the easiest electives you could take here as it's only participation and homework grades that really count. There's two tests and they're easy.
LOVED this class! I took this class as an education major with little background knowledge. She knows her stuff and made this class really fun and easy to understand. I would recommend this class.

Dr. Ault is a wonderful professor!
I really enjoyed being her class. She taught everything in a clear way, and the information stuck with me. Her expectations were always clear and she told us what was necessary to do well in the class. I hope to be in more of her classes in the future!

Overall, Dr. Ault is fantastic. She's sweet and very nice, but Iwas a little shocked by the exams. They were harder than I expected based off what she'd told us in class beforehand. Study more than you think you need to for the midterm/final, do all of the little homework assignments, and you should be able to pretty easily pull an A in the course.

Prof Smith is a great prof who truly cares about his students success. His passion for teaching is evident and he consistently goes above and beyond to ensure that the material is clear and engaging. What sets him apart is his ability to connect real-world experience with classroom concepts, making even topics easy to understand and relevant.
Professor Smith is an awful professor. He does not make sence when teaching. He is a bad grader and takes a long time to grade. I would not recoment him at all.

Prof. Smith is no doubt my favorite professor at Grove City. He is wildly experienced, cares deeply about the wellbeing of his students both personally and academically, and respects hard work in the little things. He is also engaging and makes boring topics interesting. He is truly passionate about what he does. 10/10.
Chill, Respected, thoughtful. Not incredibly hard, but just pay attention and turn things in on time.

very kind, very caring. class CAN be tough at times, but is still interesting and the term paper is not hard. instructions are always clear and she always opens her office hours up, offering help when needed!

Dr Harvey is the GOAT. Super chill guy, cares more about his students than cramming information they don't care about into their brains. He clearly knows a lot about the subject, but never takes himself too seriously. You don't need to read, or buy the books. Super easy going. Best HUMA professor at GCC, take for Civ Lit if you can.

Dr. Harvey is an approachable guy, but his class was pretty boring (I took it at 8am which didn't help). He was almost always a little late to class and pushed deadlines when needed but that meant the last couple weeks were really busy. I'm taking the final in 5 days and he still hasn't graded a single thing of mine, but most people do well.

He is one of the easier English professors. He grades participation and gives pop quizzes, but if you read it shouldn't be a problem. The participation points honestly can save you if you flunk the exams, which again aren't as bad as some of the other professors. I would for sure take Harvey if I could over some of the other profs.

Very scattered and hard to follow lectures, and LOTS of reading (~8 hours a week). Quizzes every class on the reading, but they're easy and you can get by just using sparknotes. Short debate papers at the end of every novel, but he's flexible and understanding extensions. Midterm and final are reasonable. Funny and a nice guy who loves literature,

I like Professor Harvey's style. There are a lot of readings, but the reading is manageable. If you do the readings, the regular quizzes should not be a problem. I found this to be an engaging class. Dr. Harvey focuses on classical literature more than modern literature. He is always responsive to student questions.

Not a bad guy, and a respectable professor. Not much more though, does not go above and beyond to know students or make class interesting. He's clearly just there to teach. Tough grader for the capstone class, but go to class and study for tests ahead of time and you'll be fine.

Could not tell you how frustrating this class was. The class was dry and simple yet much work with unnecessary difficulty. He is quite cold to his students. He grades very harsh on simple and easy assignments and makes it much more difficult than it needs to be. 

He requires way too much participation, and his Exams are misleading. 
Please change!

Dr. Kocur does not give clear expectations and just takes off points on everything. You are graded on whether your group does well, so other people can cause you to get an awful grade. Everything is subjective in this class so get on Kocur's good side. His class is outdated.

Dr. Kocur is very knowledgeable in the realm of business and strategic planning. His lectures are very clear and easy to follow. This class is group project based, so it is sort of paper/presentation heavy, but he is very lenient/generous with the grades he gives out. Overall, he is a very nice guy and you will actually learn a lot in this class!
He has a lot of experience actually working in business. He's passionate about the topics, and he teaches by giving real-life examples. He's not a dry professor.

I couldn't recommend Dr. Mayo more! He kept class interesting and engaging the whole time and wasn't afraid to joke with us. Overall, a very friendly and approachable guy, by far one of my favorite professors so far!

Super nice class! Lots of reading and papers to write. You'll mostly just be graded on papers so make sure you write them well. Read whatever you're assigned and say something about the reading every class; participation matters. Overall, had a fun time and got to refine my writing and comprehension skills.

An absolutely incredible professor. Makes me want to become an English major just so I can have more classes with him. He genuinely cares about his students and is one of the kindest, wisest, most intelligent professors at GCC. Even if you don't like writing, he makes it fun and engaging every class.

This man is my favorite professor and the best professor I could have asked for to start college. He's incredibly passionate about the material, and he presents it in an approachable way. He genuinely cares for his students, checking in on how we are doing, and his office is always open to talk about a paper or just to chat. 10/10 prof

He wasn't awful, but he treats everyone like they are an English major. There isn't much reading or homework but there are only like three papers and he grades them extremely hard, often with zero feedback. When I went to ask him about my grades he couldn't give a reason for a B over an A, it felt like he just didn't want to give me an A.

Not a terrible professor, definitely a funny guy. Lab was tedious at times, but that's not really his fault. He kept it interesting and humorous throughout and was definitely better than the other options. Would take again.

Dr. Clem is a fantastic teacher. He has a dry sarcasm that tends to be a turn off for some people, but personally I think he's hilarious and does an excellent job at keeping students engaged. The homework is very reasonable in length, and it makes up enough of the grade that tests aren't super heavily weighted.

This was definitely one of the best classes I've taken. Professor Clem is easily one of my favorite teachers ever. I found the class really interesting despite the tough tests and home work. Definitely would take again!

I took this class thinking it would be an easy A but I was wrong. This was a very challenging course but it really increased my knowledge about the material . It was a lot of work and it was difficult, but it was worth it. Dr. Clem is a great man; excellent humor, charismatic. However, he is not always willing to help me let alone in office hours.

If you aren't an Eskimo or part polar bear sit towards the back of the class. He leaves the windows open even in winter. He's a great professor and really entertaining. Take 207 instead of 206 its less math based.

Dr. Mac's unorganized & energetic style (nicknames, lots of clapping, jokes) made lessons hard to follow. He rushes through problems and gets frustrated when students ask questions. I felt very lost in class but it made sense when I studied on my own. Exams were manageable and he offers bonus points, but his teaching style didn't work for me.

If you like to be focused in class then McIntyre will be the most distracting part of his own class. He gets off topic easily and tells stories and jokes, which can be fun but just get to be too much sometimes. Not great at answering questions, would just restate the same thing. He does care deeply for his students though. Very fair grader.

Dr. McIntyre is a truly amazing professor.  He is my favorite at GCC so far and one of the best professors here.  He really cares about his students and wants them to succeed; be sure to visit him in office hours if you need help.  He also gives opportunities to earn back points on tests.  Can't recommend him enough!

I would run through a wall for this man. Dr. McIntyre gave me a thorough understanding of calc 3 while making it fun and making everyone laugh. One of the first classes I was genuinely sad leaving for the last time at the end of the semester. Pay attention in class and you are good to go. Do everything you can to have McIntyre for calc 3.

Dale McIntyre makes what could be a boring and difficult calc 3 class super fun and entertaining. The difficulty of the material it not too bad and is very helpful. I will actually miss this class so much!

I was nervous about taking his class because he seemed sort of intense and a little peculiar/quirky, but he turned out to be a really nice and caring person. Attendance does matter because you take a short 3-6 question quiz at the start of class on the latest reading. The readings vary; some were up to 40ish pages, but they were digestible.

Henderson is the most chill, hilarious, out of pocket, somewhat risque, dad vibe prof you will ever have. Class is super interesting, relevant, and conversation based. Relaxed environment and easy to engage with. Really feels like you're all learning together. Lots of reading and writing as well as daily quizzes, but no tests. Highly recommend!

Dr Henderson is honestly one of those guys who gives off major dad vibes, and I'm all here for it! He made class fun and cracked goofy jokes in order to keep the class engaged and awake. There was a short quiz at the beginning of every class about the reading you were meant to do for that day. Along with the journals, it's an easy class!

Dr. Henderson was great. I looked forward to his class every single week. He is entertaining, interesting, and provides phenomenal insights. There is a lot of reading but not any more than other classes at this college have. If you try and show that you've learned, you will get an A. which is how every intro class should be.

Great professor! His lectures keep you constantly engaged and you if you do the reading before hand, you'll do just fine with the quizzes. He actively engages his students in and outside of the classroom, and he makes his lectures super easy to understand and see how they fit in with the overall trajectory of the course. Also, he makes lots jokes.
didn't understand any of the material after this class

Havrilla is a kind professor and cares about her students. Her classes are tedious since she reads off the slides, but they aren't hard. The only problems I had with her class is that the wording for assignments is ambiguous so we'll lose points for something we didn't know we needed and it's hard to predict what will be on tests.
This professor asked absurd questions which were not intuitive known because of her teaching style. It was hard to find understand where she pulls her questions from.

Havrilla is a really nice Professor, but when it comes to actually teaching she is probably the worst at the school. She just reads off of the slides that she has been using for years and has very little substance to offer outside of those. The group projects do pad your grade, so you will be guaranteed at least a B+ essentially, but the tests suck

For quizzes: Copy only the bolded terms and their definitions and bring that to class. For tests: Study literally everything that was covered as well as all the bolded terms from the chapters. The tests are the hardest part of this class. For the group project: Figure out exactly what she wants and then do just that. The project is very important.

Do not be deceived by the first unit of this class. The first few weeks make you think that this class is so easy and that you don't have to study but that is false. The material feels easy until you get the exam and the questions are like nothing you have ever seen before. So, make sure you study for the exams!!!

Homans lectures are a waste of time, she does not explain anything and reads off of slides. She tries to incorporate discussions into class, but students quickly stop responding because she is extremely critical and condescending to people's responses. She is not considerate and was very rude despite unfortunate circumstances.

Research Methods is NOT a fun class with her. so so boring. there is online mindtap work, and her exams are worded so strangely it is very difficult to do well on them. we also have constant lab work and a huge paper at the end. be careful because she is very picky in what she likes and doesn't, just keep your head down and try to survive..

Dr. Homan's lectures were just reading off of the slides. However, she did provide a book with all of the slides attached. I got a B+. Not a bad grade in my opinion but I really felt like I knew the material but when it came to the tests the questions were weirdly worded making my grade lower. Overall, I think she is a good professor!

Homan's questions on her online exams are worded in a confusing way. she teaches off slides, but you can buy all the notes beforehand for class. shows lots of videos and is interactive. a little difficult-just study hard, she is a quiet professor. i would say almost a little spacy but is super caring and uses d2L for quizzes. would take again!

I don't recommend taking her if you have the option. She gives partial credit but also takes off points if there is too much work shown. Her lectures are hard to follow and if you do not read the book, you will not do well on the exams. A famous quote from her is, "everything from class is fair play for the exams" and literally every word she says.
This is actually for ACCT 303 Cost Accounting. McFeaters is a master at cost accounting, but she cannot teach it. It's mandatory so that sucks but lock in. My grade slipped QUICKLY.

FOR FNCE 302 NOT ACCT

Since it was her first time teaching, I expected some growing pains, but there were times when her teaching left us confused. She admitted to not owning or knowing how to use a financial calculator, which is essential for the course. Lectures lacked depth, had to rely on self-study or classmates.

Dr. McFeaters gives students a ton of material to be able to study for exams and quizzes, so if you are willing to put in the time (and you will need to put in the time) you'll do ok. All her accounting classes have taken a ton of time and work but she is always there to help and cares about her students A TON, even when it's hard to see sometimes

Professor McFeaters definitely cares about her students and wants everyone to succeed. Class is often boring, but that may be more due to the subject matter than to her teaching. Exams require quite a bit of studying, but there are a lot of extra resources to do extra problems for practice. She's a lot nicer in class than she is in her office hours

Dr. Marsch is a very knowledgeable professor. I found his lectures interesting and was able to pay attention even though it was a 2 hour long night class. The quizzes aren't hard since they are open book, but you'll need to study for the tests.

This man is undoubtedly the worst professor I have ever had in my entire life. He is scatterbrained, unable to lecture coherentely, he is a person that should not ever have been allowed to teach at this college. He should never be allowed back in the classroom. He is interesting as a person, but is an awful professor.
He was rude when I visited his office hours to gain clarification. He would yell at his class because he was mad when people would skip. Incredibly boring... not an interactive class.

Dr. Marsch is a super nice guy, but not my favorite professor. He is always a bit scatterbrained and quickly falls behind the syllabus schedule. The homework in this class is a pain, you have to show every tiny piece of work. He will give some extra credit on tests, but be sure you study for the them. He doesn't provide any sort of practice tests.

Marsch is a good guy and a pretty solid professor. The online homework is a bit rough but just visit the tutor or get a Chegg subscription to help you work things out. The tests aren't too bad if you really go over the lectures. He curves or does bonus in some way on exams. Grading: 3 exams + the final, online "quiz" each week plus lab grade.

I had a note from Zerbe because i got strep I emailed her and she never responded when I got back to class I told her I was sick and I emailed and infront of people pretty much belittled me and told me that zerbe notes don't count for skipping her class the next class she was sick and canceled

Dr. Baker gets a lot of hate, but I enjoyed her class. This class is hard in general, so there's not much that any prof can do to get around that. Her lectures were interesting, and our discussion was always fun. Ask her for feedback before turning papers in, and that will help you a lot.

One of the worst professors I have ever had! I had her for HIST 212, SOO SOOO much reading to do all the time and the quizzes are absolutely impossible. Unless you want this class to be your whole life's work, DO NOT TAKE!!!!

this genuinely is the hardest class i have ever taken in my life. i am a smart person, and i failed the majority of the weekly quizzes. i studied for two weeks for the midterm, it was so hard. her lectures are so hard to keep up with, everything needs written down yet the slides dont say much. i never cry over classes, but have cried over this one.

She is honestly the worst professor you could get to take this class. She never has time for questions and flies through the slide shows. The lectures are packed with information that she just assumes we know. Her voice is annoying and it was hard listening to it for an hour and 15 min.

She is awful, she is always confusing her students because she is not qualified to teach the upper level courses, she is unable to answer students questions, teaches wrong information and tests you on it, when it is wrong she will not give you points back even though that is what she taught, after figuring out what she taught was incorrect.

Professor Stone constantly confuses students in class by not explaining the material at all. She expects us to know what to do without teaching anything. Her exams are extremely confusing and they are near impossible to complete with what she gives you in class. All her work, especially the work at the beginning of the semester is just busy work.

She is one of the worst professors on this campus, despite being the chair of the accounting/finance department, she knows absolutely nothing. She can't teach at all, I have had her for 2 classes at this point and in both I have learned nothing, she gives busy work (multiple assignments a week) that is completely useless and never grades them.

I have now had this professor for three classes, and she is dreadful. You will not learn about the actual important aspects of the topics discussed in the class. She will assign a lot of work through projects/homework but won't have it graded until the last minute. She is the worst professor yet somehow the head of the accounting department.

Professor Stone does a great job communicating accounting ideas clearly. This continuation course from ACCT 201 is easy to follow as long as you understand basic accounting principles. Her exams can be challenging, but she makes it very clear what is going to be on the exam. Just stay on top of homework and use resources in connect to study.

Prof. Graybill is the GOAT, he is the only bright spot of the whole department. He explains everything clearly tries to make accounting fun and easy to understand and cares for his students he should teach higher level courses I have never heard one bad thing about him and is one of the best prof on campus I would highly recommend taking him
Its super easy but hard to understand hence why I failed.

Prof. Graybill isn't trying to make accounting hard like other professors. He wants to make sure that everyone has a genuine understanding of the material. People who have had several accounting professors usually speak highly of him.

Graybill is the goat. He is not there to make you hate accounting, and he does his best to make sure you genuinely understand the material. The only way to fail his class is to either not show up to class, not do work, or not try. Probably one of if not the best teacher I've had at Grove.

Professor Graybill is an amazing teacher who has real world experience and cares about his students. He is super accessible outside of class to talk about both academic and personal matters. Accounting is a little boring, but he makes the topic interesting and walks through tons of examples. He's one of my favorite profs at Grove.

Hutchins is extraordinarily accommodating toward his students. I write this the same day he both extended the deadline for our second program and moved our exam back because students in class said they had two other exams the same week. He is (ridiculously) available for consultation during and outside of office hours. He wants students to succeed.
His office hours will transform your mind. Excellent man. Really cares about his pupils.

Dr. Hutchins CARES about his students (free donuts 4 finals TT). He'd never break their hearts by making exams harder than needed, providing lots of sample code. No quizzes. Least hw to learn enough Java. Ok lectures. Accessible, caring office hours. Group work in class. Pick a good table for the semester 1st week. Be logical and you'll do great.

Dr. Hutchins is okay at lecturing, but his strong suit is helping students in office hours. I asked a lot of questions, and he always was excited to help and provided helpful tips on how to figure things out myself. Overall I think he's a great teacher if you pay attention in class, and he's more willing to help than most other professors.
Dr. Hutchins is absolutely amazing. Would highly recommend taking any of his classes.

Dr. McCray was amazing. Civ Lit was a great amount of reading, nothing too crazy. She is very passionate about the books, will answer any questions, and is genuinely my favorite professor at GCC so far. Class is a mix of lecture and small group discussions. This is my first class of the day, and I never struggle to get up and ready for the class!!!

Well, he is vexing. He is probably the smartest professor at the school but the problem is he lets everyone know it. His lectures are amazing and he has good explanations for almost every question. However, he forgets he is teaching students and expects everyone to be of the same intelligence level as him. Great professor, insufferable personality.

Had this professor a long time ago, I had a 4.0 GPA and he attempted to blame his poor teaching on his students, which is hilarious. Retook his class with a real professor and got an A. He'll try to say you didn't read his instructions but in reality he's just not good at teaching.
not as difficult as everyone says, very fair

Drop this class. I don't mind pop quizzes and tests but that's pretty much it and the material is overwhelming..vague in class..really not sure what we are trying to learn. My GPA was great until this class. Not sure if its control he is needing, or a class on how to teach.

I took Dr. Verbois for a summer HUMA 200 class. His lectures easily kept my attention and with his guidance the class had insightful discussions. The class is difficult, since there is so much material to memorize but he lets you know what sections of material will be on the test.

Dr Dixon is a frustrating professor to have. He lectures once every couple of weeks but gives you large homework packets that require a lot of work full of concepts that he hasn't taught. We are supposed to use the textbook but it often has just a couple of sentences to describe a difficult concept that we are then expected to understand.
Professor Dixon was difficult to reach outside of class and I barely knew how to code throughout the entire class.

Dr. Dixon is a fantastic man. Have conversations with him about the course material and also his career and life. As far as MATLAB, he gives a significant amount of homework and practice exams before each test. That being said, do not get too confident after the first exam. You have to know the stuff inside and out to be able to succeed after it.

Dr. Dixon is a decent professor. His lectures are helpful if you've never programmed before, but they're a little boring if you have. He assigns quite a bit of homework which tends to be straightforward. The quizzes are on numerical computing topics, most of which are never used again in the class. Dixon is an adequate professor overall.

One of the best of the engineering professors at Grove City that I've met so far. He can be strict but it is obvious that he truly cares about the progress of his students. He teaches very well and overall he is a great guy. I hope I'll have many more classes with him.
His tests were not similar to what he taught during class so if you don't do his review you are screwed.

His style of teaching is not organized and therefore I found his lectures difficult to understand. He also gave us frequent homework assignments without teaching the necessary information to complete them. He is clearly a genius and a very knowledgeable man, but in my experience, he does not communicate that knowledge very effectively.
Dr Thompson was passionate about the course, explained the concepts well, and gave great feedback on tests and quizzes. Little homework, so grade was more test dependent.

Calc 1 with Dr. Thompson would have been much more difficult if I did not have a calc background. I do not recommend taking him if have zero calc knowledge because he moves fast. However, he is very nice and is extremely helpful during office hours. He holds a test review sessions that are great and you should attend if you want to do well.
Professor Thompson is nice and explained some things well but I still couldn't completely understand the subject.
