She is very good at what she does and is passionate about Anatomy in class and in lab :)
Insightful class. I learned so much. Really made me think about public health impact in the world.
You have to memorize everything in Dr. Farone's class but it is very doable if you put the effort in and she has a very fascinating way of presenting the information. I loved taking her class.

Professor Farone is a nice enough person that it very knowledgeable about the subject. That being said, she can be condescending at times and makes her exams unnecessarily difficult, tainting one's view of the class. Her office hours were also extremely limited making it challenging to get help.
Dr. Farone helped me through things both personal and professional. Shes gives real caring advice. Her classes were most relevant to my career interests.

I've enjoyed taking Principles of Accounting with Professor Shultz. The handouts given in class were extremely helpful and covered a lot of the material. She was also always available outside of class and always ready to answer any questions I had. As long as you pay attention and put effort into understanding the homework you will be fine.

Professor Shultz was a nice person but as a professor she was pretty bad. She was very disorganized, never graded our quizzes or papers until the last class (even though we asked countless times), she also was very lecture heavy that were pretty unengaging for students. Overall would not recommend taking her class.

Professor Shultz is a lovely human being. She is still somewhat new at teaching. she can explain most things more fully in office hours. Don't mess around in her class--pay attention. Connect homework are easy and guaranteed "A". Study for tests well. Manual assignments are graded on correctness not completion.
Hard to pay attention. Most of material is self-taught.
not great at teaching but hey, you'll get a a

I would highly recommend choosing any other religion professor. I found that the books assigned taught me much more than Shepson himself. I have taken Bibza and Hall, and I would recommend both of them over him. It is not worth the 3 credits to lose your sanity in his class. He also made outlandish claims about a student in front of other students.

If you want a traditional learning experience, you should consider a different professor. Doc is unlike many teachers in that he understands the value of experiential learning and implements it into the class. If you want to be challenged academically and religiously, absolutely take him. Challenging, but only lazy people want to do easy things.
Dr. Shepson assigns so many papers its, funny you'll find yourself having to do five papers in the course of a week and a half all being 3-8 pages long. The worst experience ever!

Dr. Shepson is kind and genuinely cares for his students. He's an easy grader but his class is a LOT of work per week. It can be hard to know what to write down during lectures and his slides and study guide were too vague to be helpful study materials. If taking for the BARS elective, I would recommend taking World Religions with Dr. Byun instead.

I took Biblical Revelation as a freshmen. This class was incredibly hard for a freshmen and he gave little to no background information for those who didn't know the Bible well. I did not enjoy his lectures and his slides look like they were made in the 1900s. Very boring... take someone else.

WOW! Dr. Munson is so passionate about his subject, and it shows. Yes, his CivArts class does focus more on music than the others, but he teaches in a way that makes it accessible to non-music people. Just lock in and listen attentively + take good notes! His class was light in the midst of a very dark semester for me. Live, laugh, love Dr. Munson!

Dr. Munson is a passionate lecturer who knows his material well. However, he is a very picky grader. There's a particular way he wants his answers written and it can take you a long time to learn exactly what he wants. Have fun music majors!! You'll definitely bond with your class over Thursday Big Quizzes :')

If you don't need to take this class to graduate, what are you even doing? If you do need it, buckle up. He is constantly talking about how he wants us to succeed and yet he puts songs on the test that were not on the syllabus and schedules exams for the day after break. This is the most difficult class you will ever take. It's awful.

Dr. Munson is a magnificent professor. I hung on his every word in lectures, and proudly fought for an A in his class. Rest assured, it can be done, but you have to want it, and you have to earn it. But please, listen to what he's teaching about the music and the art. That information will transform your appreciation of the arts. It did for me.

This professor expected too much of general education students. The art he chose to teach was completely biased to what he liked and much was not useful or insightful. His exams are ridiculously hard for it being required by the school. He provided little grace when it came to grading.

I must say she is nice and will interact with students, but she is the worst teacher I have had at Grove City. She makes no sense half the time and the TAs explained things 10x better than she did. Honestly if they were the ones teaching the class it would have been a breeze. Asking her questions typically gets you nowhere as well.
Better off never asking her a question because it will never be explained clearly. She talks in hieroglyphics half the time. Shes nice tho just can't teach. Gives extra credit which is nice.

Wagner is a super nice person, but is not very good at teaching. Her lectures are boring and almost impossible to follow. I essentially had to teach myself the material. Her tests are also extremely difficult.

She is an alright teacher. Not the best at lecturing, but helpful in office hours. She gives a large flat curve with an opportunity to make up some missed points on tests. Those who study, pay attention in class, and use the problem solving method she gives you can expect to do very well.

YOU WILL NOT GET ANYTHING OUT OF THIS COURSE OTHER THAN 4 CREDITS 
IF YOU WANT TO LEARN SOMETHING TAKE BROWER you may not get any extra credit or curve but you will know more and end with the same grade as long as you put in the work
I guess if you didn't take him, you'll never know.
Awesome teacher, Would love to take again for his quirky humor unfortunatelyhe is leaving Grove City as of 2025

Dr. Powell is a phenomenal professor! His lectures were so engaging and the highlight of my day. He is so passionate about what he does and truly cares for his students. However, watch out for the pop quizzes and tests because they can be difficult!! Do the reading, study the bold terms, and pay close attention in lectures!

Dr. Powell is an amazing prof! His lectures are funny and informative and never boring. His tests are tough, but this class is absolutely worth it. He is funny and genuine and makes marketing interesting to everyone. 10/10 would recommend even though he's a tough grader.

A second-semester Junior and he is still the best professor I've taken, by far. There's a reason he's rated 4.9. Years of experience and is an extremely gifted lecturer. I could still give a 30-minute rant about what I learned Freshman year. Take him every chance you get. His tests are harder than most, but an A is likely if you study the terms.
"If you miss any points, I will bring in SPAGHETTI, and hit you with spaghetti!" also, "I am in the church of doing cocaine!". I mean come on. What a legend.

Very lecture heavy, and depending on length and time of the class, it can be difficult to pay attention. Not a bad class, the content was just kind of boring. He's a really nice guy though and takes the time to know all the students and their interests. Most of the tests could be taken with a notecard, which made it definitely possible to do well.

Regarding how difficult his courses are - they're usually not easy. However, he's a required professor for most business classes, so there's no avoiding it. Hey though, don't fret - this guy in some classes let students test with a notecard of notes, and his exams arent so hard that you cant pass. Some of you might be able to pass w/o much studying

The class was interesting overall and he did a great job teaching it! I got bored watching the video lectures (took GBI as a summer online course) but I was able to maintain the material better than any class I've taken on campus so far!
Markley was one of my favorite professors this semester. As long as you keep up with the homework videos which aren't bad at all the class isn't very hard and extremely interesting. Great class.
Helped me fall in love with biology after I switched my major
Very particular and gives study guides for exams that are not useful. Very dry would not take him again.
Overall good class, and does not accept late work. I had a learning curve with the GIS application because I'm not computer savvy. Don't be afraid to ask him questions, he is very helpful

I did not like this professor at all. He seems to never like answers to his questions given in class. Nothing is ever quite right for him. He ignores some of his students and loves others. If you aren't pumped up about ecology, he will not like you. Not enjoyable at all.

She's hard to approach at first but once you get to know her, she's great! I would say you definitely can't go through this course putting in little effort. To get an A or an A-, it requires blood, sweat, and tears but it is such a rewarding class, I learned so much!

Two baby science courses are required for GCC gen eds. I'm not a science minded person and really struggled in this class, but Dr. Cramer is an amazing person. I  utilized her office hours and spent a lot of time getting help outside of class. Don't be afraid to ask her for support or let her know you've fallen behind. She actually cares!
Dr. Cramer will definitely push you, but she also cares very much and grades fairly so that your grades reflect the amount of work you put in.

Dr. Cramer cares about her students, clearly understands the material she teaches in OChem II, and lectures at a manageable pace.  The class is very difficult, but it's supposed to be that way--there's no such thing as easy OChem.  For heaven's sake, don't leave negative reviews just because you failed the class.  Dr. Cramer is a wonderful person.

organic chemistry is inherently difficult for most. Dr. Cramer is a good teacher, tests are fair. Dont get behind, do practice questions early. Helpful in office but not a tutor. Reaction notebook was helpful. I did average in this course, but it prepared me for MCAT - organic/bio was my best section and I got into med school. Thanks Dr. Cramer.

I've had Dr. Stauff for both Cell Bio and Immunology, and I'm taking his Micro course later. Immunology was an excellent course - the lectures were informative, his tests really challenged you to understand the material in context, and I learned a lot!

Dr. Stauff is a very good teacher. I really disliked the subject matter but he made his exams doable if you studied, despite not liking the class, and he had a lot of lab work that made the grade balanced and not extremely exam heavy. While I would never take his class again, I would take him again if he taught something else.

Brilliant professor, but knows how to convey information in such a way that students understand and find the material interesting. Hated biology before this class, but now I find it fascinating. Knows how to use ppt to make lectures fun. Has great examples and a wonderful sense of humor. Use his study guides and you'll do very well
Dr. Stauff is the least pretentious and most brilliant professor I've had. So down to earth and patient but you can tell he's passionate about his work and helping students.

Dr. Stauff is a brilliant educator, funny, kind and humble. Youd better study hard for his tests, but the material comes from his excellent powerpoints. Use textbook for clarification. Micro was great prep for med school. Thanks Dr. Stauff.
I love love love Dr. Barbour!! She's such a sweet person :)
Dr. Barbour is really sweet. Her lectures can be a little dry, and the accountability assignments are annoying, but she makes it easy to succeed. The exams are exactly how she tells you they will be.
Boring, but it's hard to make Civ Lit interesting. One of the easier profs for Civ Lit, since the reading quizzes are due before class, not in class

I had the busiest semester of my life and studied 30 minutes for both the midterm and final for her class. I don't think any teacher should make their exams so easy that you don't really need to study for them. On the other hand, if you study hard you are assured an A+ in the class. She also inserts her political views frequently and is boring.

I really appreciate the fact that this class was easy, I really do. As a non-English major taking difficult literature or writing classes is something that I very much dislike. However the line between easy and boring was crossed in this class. The highlight of this class was watching the dude sitting in front of me lose money gambling online.

I honestly loved Dr. Welton's class. He is very kind and helpful. I recommend taking it with a friend and making the most of his stories and subtle jokes. There are 4 exams (practice tests) and 3 graded homework assignments (he will check them if you go to office hours). The online textbook is expensive and I stopped using it mid-semester.

the most boring class i've ever taken. no point in even going to class because he doesn't even explain the topics and you will end up teaching everything to yourself in your little spare time. he is helpful if you go to his office hours though. study a lot for his exams and use the powerpoints. the textbook doesn't really help

Difficult class if you do not pay attention or use the other sources he gives for help. He is knowledgeable, accessible, and a fair grader. Does curve well. Tells you what will be on test to study. Tests are hard but not impossible. Very monotone and boring lectures, but he's a nice guy. Extremely passionate about stats. You'll be fine if you try.

Gary Welton was lecture based and his teaching methods were subpar. He was incredibly monotone and the class ended up being the most boring 45 minutes of my MWF. Unfortunately, he is the only professor who teaches this class. His lack of enthusiasm in teaching led me to often get off track and it was difficult to remain attentive during class.
I haven't learned anything in this class. It is so incredibly boring I almost fall asleep. His slides make no sense. He like makes his own stuff up to?
He is my favorite professor. I highly recommend taking any class of his.
Know the material to avoid pop-quizzes. His lectures were informative, and Biddle is an excellent resource on HR matters, as he's been in HR over 20 years. I would take him again in a heartbeat!

This was a great course I would recommend anyone taking it. Dr. Biddle has extensive knowledge in this field and it shows. He takes you through scenarios that he's gone through and hypothetical ones. It helped me get a taste of real-world experiences

I took it over the summer and I just fell too far behind because it was a lot of work, but he was overly gracious and gave me extensions for months so I could pass the class. He took off points for being late, but I still passed. He's a relatively standard professor, but his understanding and ability to work with you sets him apart.

Dr. Biddle is my all-time favorite Grove City Professor. I'm not great at taking exams, but his are super easy. Further, all the content he tests on is online, so you can find quizlets to study for any class he teaches. His personality seems dry initially, but the man is a walking example of weird combined with funny and knowledgeable.

I had Kelley for MNGT 101, and he helped me switch my major to one I love. I'm jealous of anyone who has him as him as a professor since he's my favorite person on this campus. He cares deeply for every student and wants to get to know the joys and struggles of their lives. He commuted 2 hours every day but had the joy of the Lord, even at 8am.

This class is great. Dr. Kelley makes the class very engaging, and the material is interesting as well. There are 15 chapter quizzes and 2 exams. Overall, this class isn't very difficult, and I looked forward to every class.

By far one of my favorite professors. At least with MNGT 103 there is a fair amount of group work, however none of it is actually difficult.

He has fairly open office hours and is great to talk to, even if not related to class.

My only "negative" note (not negative to me, though), is that his voice can be a bit monotone occasionally.

Prof. Butler is a nice guy and a pretty good teacher. His class was interesting, and he's big on participation. There was reading you were supposed to do before class, and he would cold call on you for it. He's a harsh grader, looking for specific phrases and ideas and didn't give any extra credit.

Professor Butler is a very informative professor. He has practical experience in the international business field, and is very suited to teach it. Even for being an afternoon class, I was always engaged. Getting an "A" is very doable if you put in the effort.

Professor Butler is one of the best professors I have ever had.  Amazing and engaging lectures and they do not make you zone out.  Really laid back and really easy and fun to talk to.  EXTREMELY KNOWLEDGEABLE in the field and very easy to get a hold of and talk to.  All around great guy and amazing professor.  11/10 would recommend him to anyone.
One of the best professors I have had at GCC. The only HUMA I enjoyed and got something out of. Very thought-provoking and interesting lectures.

I was told that I have to take a class with Byun and I don't regret it. He is funny and super approachable. His lectures are so interesting I always leave his class thinking about something he said especially on Genesis. Most of the exams are from the lectures. I definitely hope to take more classes with him!

Be warry that everything Byun teaches or has an opinion on may end up on the midterm or final. Quizzes will require studying for if you want good grades on them. He's a very reasonable grader when it comes to papers or in class writing. Overall, fantastic class.
Tough class but definitely worth it!

Dr. Byun is incredible! He's probably one of the most knowledgeable people you'll ever meet, and each one of his lectures is jam packed with fascinating information. He's super passionate about the material he teaches and his enthusiasm definitely  rubs off on his students. Highly recommend taking this class.

One thing that trips me up with this guy is how fast he talks. It's sometimes difficult to understand what he's saying but I am absolutely not saying that's a reason not to take his class. Sure, his quizzes and exams are difficult, but if you practice the material, you can do well.

Professor Johnson is a wonderful professor. He makes clear what will be on quizzes and exams, and the assignments are fun. He is also incredibly accessible outside of class, I've gone to office hours at 8pm before and he was happy to explain everything I wanted to know.
Don't buy the textbook for the class, he has all the information on the sides.

I've taken multiple classes with Johnson and I've enjoyed all of them. This was his first semester teaching at GCC, but he did great. He put countless hours into making new slides and was always available to answer questions on Teams (even at 2:30am lol). As he gets more experience, I only expect his classes to get better!
Dr. Yowler is a skr8 G. Yes, genetics is a little difficult and exams are hard, but Yowler (and Dr. A) are both amazing teachers.

Seems intimidating at first but is actually super nice and down to earth. Very realistic and straightforward with students both about class expectations/hw and test info and what to expect further on in the field irl. Also very understanding and encouraging with students who are struggling.
A rating this high on a website were students only come to complain should speak for itself
Yowler's tests are hard. He is really funny and will meet with you to help you. If you try, you can do good.

Dr. Yowler made genetics interesting and was a great prof. Your final is a group project that is super easy, and you get to drop your lowest test score which is amazing. ALWAYS go to recitation, those problems will be most of your exam sometimes verbatim. If you don't understand a concept just ask he is super nice.
The class was fine, he just stated his opinions. He doesnt have a clear grading criteria, especially for participation. He also really likes red heads for some reason…
says WOOF a lot

Campbell is a somewhat good professor, however at some points it can feel boring. For exams you have to write a paper which is an easy 20% of the total exam grade, however the questions in the exams are not for overthinkers, a mixture of answers like "two of these are correct." For true or false, I found that 80% of the time it is true, not false.
Let's be real, most people at GCC don't like taking HUMA classes. Take Campbell. He's simple and easy. Kinda boring, but most of the HUMA's are. You'll pass this class I gurantee it.

Dr. Campbell is a very nice man, but an interesting teacher. He is very set in his ways and somewhat extreme. A lot of his class is his book and his opinion, not fully the Bible. His tests are extremely difficult and worth a large chunk of your grade, but the quizzes and other paper can help boost your grade. Lots of reading. Kind of a mid class.

I am normally very prone to zoning out, but his lectures kept my attention throughout. He manages to put a lot of information into each lecture while remaining clear and comprehensible, as well as unbiased. His expectations for the class are also made clear.

The lectures were great, super interesting & enjoyable content. But the tests were very difficult: no multiple choice, just a bunch of short/long answers (with multiple parts). If you didn't remember something small, that was probably -%10. Also the group essays/projects were time-consuming and kinda hard. But I enjoyed the lectures at least.

Awful prof. Makes his exams impossible and incredibly difficult to note take in class. Wants no part in student-teacher relationship, not personable at all, does not care if you pass or fail the class. Avoid at all costs if possible.

The exact opposite of Al Moakar. Absolutely fantastic in all respects with consideration to a professor. Had him for 4 years and is the best professor I've ever had in my college experience. Uses class time extremely well/efficiently.

Absolutely fantastic professor. Really cares about his students and wants them to learn. Homework is always directly related to course material and lectures are very interesting. Complete opposite of Dr. Al Moakar.
Dr. Waha is so sweet! Her class was always really fun and the discussion was engaging. She is super workable and wants you to succeed! If she is an option, take her!

I love Dr. Waha. She's really passionate about the material, and it shows in her teaching. She balances well discussion and lectures, too. Her tests aren't easy, but she does prepare you well for them.

She is one of the better professors at this school. Obviously there is a lot of reading, but it's an English course so that's a given. She grades fairly and offers clear feedback. If she is an option I would take her over other professors. The class is easier than others and she is very nice and helpful. I cannot say the same for other professors.
Professor Waha is a kind professor. She is always willing to help me better understand the material. I recommend that students take her for this course.

I took Dr. Waha for many classes; she was always very kind and knowledgeable. She is prompt in all her grading and offers detailed feedback on papers. Her paper grading rubric is most helpful. Reading quizzes are easy if you do the rereading. Tests are extremely reasonable as long as you put in the studying time. Could not recommend more highly!
Horrible. Definitely not even close to being an historian. Stick to psychology and let the real historians teach American history.

Dr. Throckmorton is one of the only reasons I have liked GCC. He actually has a balanced perspective on the world and truly cares about individual students. He is deeply kind and wise, and just a truly great person. His classes are easy to ace if you just pay attention. 10/10 recommend.

He is literally the one thing I liked about the school. His classes were my favorite and he is also just a great guy. Amazing prof and really knows what he's talking about. (especially in Foundations of Counselling)

Dr. Warren Throckmorton is one of the very best professors at this school. He cares for his students and wants to see us succeed. He's very reassuring and easy to talk to. Don't hesitate to reach out for help, because he's the nicest guy I've ever met. His lectures are beyond informative and his exams are clear and straight forward. 10/10 recommend
Throckmorton was one of my favs. He's very accommodating and provides more than enough extra credit to get a good grade.

Overall, Zhang is a good professor. He is very accessible outside of class (even outside of office hours) and he cares a lot about his students. His tests and quizzes aren't bad either. The only major issue with this class was the projects. They are very unclear and have little relevance to what we talk about in class. Going to office hours is key.
What can you say? This guy is funny, engaging, and really cares about his students.
I had Dr. Falcetta in CHEM 112 (lab) and he is one of my favorite professors! He is very caring and helpful. He explains things clearly and concisely, and his office hours are very helpful!

This man is an angel. He makes it very possible to succeed. Plenty of extra credit, very good study guides for exams, and he is hilarious. There is a lot of homework, but if you put in the work, you should have no problem doing well. He was my favorite teacher my first semester, and I really wish I could take a class with him again.

With pre-lecture videos/quizzes & post-lecture homework, expect about 1.5 to 2 hours of homework per class. Class time is spent doing practice problems, but Falcetta does an excellent job at walking you through them and explaining the concepts. Labs are relatively short and easy. The subject is difficult, but Falcetta is always available to help.
He is hilarious and a great teacher who gives all of the resources you need to succeed.

Great teacher, super talented. Apparently, he threw a chair at a kid who showed up late once though, so I made an effort never to show up late. He's a bit flamboyant, but I think that adds to his class.
first week of class: will throw a chair at you if you are late the rest of the year: best professor you ever had, will bring joy into your life every T/TR
PERCUSSION METHODS WAS THE HIGHLIGHT OF MY WEEK EVERY WEEK. HAPPY TUESDAY. love this guy, made class so much fun, 100000/10 would reccommend.

He's a great teacher but as the other reviewers have said, DO NOT BE LATE. He freaked out big time once but after (and before) that he's just a really cool guy and a lot of fun to talk to. Very clear in teaching and a very good percussionist. Definitely take him.
FANTASTIC Percussionist.  Just do not EVER be late or drop your sticks.  He will glare at you and make you want to crawl in a hole.  Overall though, he is fantastic! Very down to earth.

Sports Ministry is an easy 3 credits. The class is all based around lectures adn the papers he gives you, so attendance is a must. Walked out of the final in 30 minutes, got an A. Class is easy IF YOU STUDY. It's not that hard. Tests are a TON of writing so stretch your hand out before so you don't cramp or somethin I dunno
Thrasher's classes are very manageable if you pay attention and attend class. He tells you what to expect on exams. I had him for sports ministry and sports ethics and would definitely recommend.
Dr. Thrasher cares a lot about his students; only a midterm and a final and three papers.

I count myself fortunate to have taken Bib Rev with Dr. Thrasher. He is a genuine person who is passionate about his subject. There are only two tests, which can be good or bad depending on how you look at it. He even has outlines for his lectures. Word to the wise, vocab will make or break your grade in this class.

Dr Thrasher is amazing! I highly recommend his Intro to Sports Ministry class, especially if you love sports. He cares about his students, helps you grow as a Christian athlete, isn't unreasonable in his assignments, and is a fair grader. NOTE: He didn't give us any quizzes (which was nice), and the textbooks, while beneficial, weren't really used.

Now I'm no Grover, but I got an A+ in his class, so that must be saying something. He's a great teacher for stats and I learned a lot from him. Just pay close attention and do the homework's until you fully understand the concepts. It's all based around Excel, so it is way easier than you might think.
This class isn't really a math class. It's basically Excel but with a more math centered approach. Dr Smith will meet with you about class but was also very kind outside of class. Would recommend.

There's a lot of hate here about Professor Smith, which I think is unwarranted. Yes, his lectures are very fast-paced, but they are still manageable. 
Attending his review sessions is absolutely essential! They were such a huge help. Absolutely attend them!!!
Thank you for such a great class Professor Smith!
Explains concepts well and is willing to go the extra mile to help you understand.

Dr. Smith is a solid professor. He gives homework that prepares students well for the exams. In my experience, exams are graded with some leeway and emphasis on partial credit. Dr. Smith's shortcomings come in his ability to actually teach the material. His slides are not his own, and he skips a lot of the material making it hard to take notes.

Professor Knupp is a really great Spanish teacher. She cares for her students and helps you learn no matter how difficult the subject. She will speak in Spanish but give you time after each sentence to reflect and think through what exactly she just said. Learn the vocab and study for indirect and direct pronouns and you'll easily pass
Best spanish teacher. She makes spanish exciting. tests and quizzes are easy. There was a lot of homework but thats in all of the spanish classes at Grove.

Great class. She was very clear with what she expected us to know and how to succeed in her class. Easy A if you're willing to put in the work. Also, for the cultura sections on the tests just literally memorize the entire slide that she gives you so that you don't have to worry about losing those points on the tests.

Knupp is an amazing professor and cares very much for her students. Her exams are tricky, but if you nail down each concept you'll do great. Her office hours are definitely worth going to, and she genuinely wants you to succeed. During my oral final exam, I put in extra effort and she could tell, which helped me to get an A for that exam.

Never taken Spanish, but she made sure to clear things up, you just have to speak up in her classes. She knows how to teach as she is a past high school teacher. She is a tough grader and her quizzes and test are not easy but she does make study guides with clear expectations. If you are actively involved/volunteer in class, then you will be ok.
She's awesome!

This was the hardest class I have taken, but that was not because of the material. I essentially had to teach everything to my self. In class she would only work out examples half way; the homework she assigned didn't match what we covered in class, and the homework didn't match the quizzes or exams. Dr. Bonomo should not teach Calculus.

Dr. Bonomo is a good person, and she's genuinely trying, but her class is not good. Her calc sections are the most behind of the whole department, and she doesn't cover content thoroughly. Her tests and quizzes don't really match the homework or classwork, and her lectures aren't super clear. I'd be screwed if I hadn't taken calc before.

I did not enjoy this class. I love math and have always done well, but this class was rough. There are quizzes every week on material we spent 15 mins going over. The quizzes were short, but the points add up. The exams were fine, but the final was insanely hard. The class was struggling and nothing changed the whole semester. Pretty bummed!

You have to work to get a good grade, but she is very accessible and grades very fairly. Her dry sense of humor is amazing. I don't love group projects so I wasn't looking forward to that but hers take place in class and are the most stress-free I've ever done.
i liked him a lot but he grades his papers harshly in my opinion. his lectures are really interesting and he's pretty entertaining. exams can be tricky idk make sure you keep up with the reading
Kind! Kind lovely man! Everyone loves Dr. Harp!

Has an attendance sheet. I liked his lectures and his dry humor. He is very knowledgeable and is a pretty likeable guy. If you like history, you'll like him. If not, you might not. He is a little bit vague when it comes to assignments and you will probably have to talk to him a lot to help out with that stuff.

a great and super nice guy. really clearly cares and wants to help students. looks for grammar on papers, so be careful. overall class can be a little bit boring, but you will definitely learn. he challenges perceptions and if you engage with the material, you will really learn a lot

A lot of people will say that Harp's lectures are boring, and they can be a bit dry, but if you like history you'll like his style and dry humor. Really tough grader, and you aren't graded by very much. Make sure you are ready for the essays on the exams, because the multiple choice usually aren't too bad.
she's really sweet, if a little disorganized. her class was pretty easy but with a decent amount of reading. lots of quizzes. midterm & final boost your grade if you prepare well

This course wasn't too bad. Lots of reading. Your grade will rely on quizzes, so you have to understand and remember what you read. Midterms and finals will make up a lot of your grade. Besides all that, this course gives you the opportunity to learn about history, drama, and yourself. I'd recommend if you're into drama or literature.
Dr. Craig is not clear in her instructions and does not answer questions well. She rarely checks her email and takes forever to grade assignments.

Betsy Craig gives you a grade and doesn't really explain her reasoning behind it. She has VERY high expectations for the students in her class and can be very intimidating at times. All in all she is passionate about what she teaches, but I would not take this class if you are just looking for an easy extra class.

Dr Craig is a horrible professor. Her lectures are out of touch & boring. My first speech was initially an B+, which she later switched to a C- and worked my grades up to As - all to make herself look better by showing my "progress" in the class. She is unfair and unlikable, and is more focused on her plays and shows than the classes she teaches.

Dr. Potter is legendary among the English majors at Grove City! Poetry was a most enjoyable and memorable class, to be sure. While feedback is helpful, expect the exams to be writing dense and critically graded. Essentially, you will get out of the class what you put into it. :)

Dr Potter's civ lit class was more difficult and required more effort than other professors but he is really interesting. Has pop quizzes but they are really easy. Exam questions and essays were kind of hard.

Dr. Potter is one of those tough love professors that really pushes you to write better and doesnt sugarcoat anything. He is humorous at times, and opens every class with a Biblical proverb or poem. He never uses rubrics, making things a bit unclear, but he is accessible

Solid choice for intersession! Dr. Potter understood that we weren't English majors, and didn't force an obscene amount of work on us, and graded us fairly. He does make a lot of weird comments about bad roommates though, maybe some horrid past experience?

Took Creative Writing online with Dr. Potter. He's kind and encouraging, and the class isn't hard per se, but it can be difficult to ascertain the specifics of what you need to do to truly succeed. In terms of the online course, the syllabus is basically all you get for story/poetry instructions (no rubric), so reach out if you need clarification.

I had Dr. Culbertson for Issues in Education. She is nit-picky when it comes to grading and usually always takes off for small mistakes. However, she has a lot of experience and is very insightful. This class had a hefty amount of work but it was a nice seminar.

Dr. Culbertson is incredibly personable and cares about her students so deeply. She does all that she can to be open and real with her classes, as well as prepare them for the teaching world outside of the college classroom.

Dr. Culbertson was hands down my favorite professor in the classroom and out. She pours her heart into teaching and all her students. Her classes are very informative and she gives clear expectations. Anything you need you can go to her to ask questions, she is an amazing listener! You will LOVE her!!

Dr. Culbertson was my student teaching supervisor. She also organizes all student teachers. She is organized, understanding, and a WEALTH of knowledge, feedback, and advice. She made my student teaching experience not only memorable, but unforgettable. I loved my other advisor, but I was really sad to leave her. If you get her, you're lucky!

Best Ed. class at GCC. Dr. C was a principal, so she's a great resource. She prepares you for grad school, too-she's the only prof that actually took the time to teach us APA formatting. Your perspective will change a lot because you'll get to form your own opinion on controversial issues. Made me a great interview candidate and a better teacher!
Dr. Nichols is my favorite professor here. She is always excited to teach and loves to help her students understand content. 10/10.
Fantastic professor and person. Cares for students on an academic and personal level. Extremely helpful and insightful.

The most fun class I have taken! The teacher is actively engaged and has the class laughing in seconds of class. We learn a lot but we are having a good time while learning. We play pranks and jokes on one another and support one another outside the classroom when we have to miss class for sports.

This class is majority based on discussions, lectures, & presentations. Dr. Nichols does an awesome job of allowing her students to lead class talks about controversial issues in the world today. You will work on a project throughout the entire semester, have short papers here and there, and do small assignments. She is the best!
She's the absolute best at GCC!

Dr Kengor is the best professor at grove city. He makes class interesting and you actually learn so much. His class is honestly a break because he doesn't overload you with work but if you come to class you will learn probably more than you do in your classes where you have a ton of assignments

Usually 5 min. late to class. Lots of class time are spent on anecdotes and tangents but they're usually fun. He's huge on laissez-faire economics and the comparative part of the class focuses heavily on that. I'm not very right-wing on economics and it wasn't a problem with grading or anything. And it's Grove City College, what did you expect?

Dr. Kengor is by far the most interesting prof I've had. Sure he can go off on tangents as some students have noted, but you always leave feeling as if you've learned a lot about the Middle East. He's always more than happy to give advice on the assignments and easy to reach after class. Can't recommend enough!
Great lecturer, understanding about missed classes, tests were fair, ability and understanding based not memorization based. Hates communism for sure.
Kenny G is the man. Fun and interactive speaker, very knowledgeable, one of my favorite profs.
Fun interesting class with an amazing, informed professor. If you don't understand anything just go to his office hours and he will make everything right. Chill guy, great professor.
Mark Fair is a pretty reasonable teacher. Very helpful in office hours. Actually cares about his students. Just a chill guy if you get to know him. He isn't a super easy grader but he grades FAIRly.

Material Science with Dr. Fair was a tough class, but the content was interesting and Dr. Fair did an excellent job delivering it. Homework and exams make up your entire grade in this class so make sure to be able to do problems he does in class and on the homework to prepare for the tests. Focus a little bit on the vocabulary but mostly problems.

Best professor in the ME department. Took him whenever possible. His exams are reasonable. Explains things very well and gives you what you need to do hw and exams. Was always giving equations to my friends in dr. Claus's sections since she skipped what they needed.
Dr. Fair is the FAIR-est of them all!!!!

I LOVE Dr. A so much. She is so clear with teaching and is so caring. She will meet with you and wants you to succeed. She will do anything she can to help you out. I have learned so much in her class.

Dr. A is an amazing professor. Her lectures are engaging and even though the class had probably fifty students, she knew each one of us by name. That class was also the best-structured class I have ever taken. There was an exam every two weeks so it was not an overload of material on each one, and we worked on the final project all semester!
Amazing teacher my favorite professor on campus. Genetics is a hard class but I enjoyed learning the material, she's very helpful in office hours explains everything very well.

Dr. A is the best professor I have ever had. She knows the material so well and explains it ways that help you to understand it. She helped me understand tendencies about myself to help my studying techniques. She records her lectures so you can listen to the class over again. If you do well on the exams, you can drop your final. She is the best.

Dr. A is amazing. I took this class to fill my science credit and she knows that she has non-science minded people. She really helps her students succeed and truly cares about her students. She is really clear with her material and expectations. Dr. A is a fantastic lady and excellent professor.

this class is rlly boring. she's a kind teacher but the difficulty of the course is still there and the exams she gives are mad thorough. need to be prepared to study art for hours and she wants very specific responses on the exams. not a very fun class but at least she tries to crack jokes and is supportive outside of class

Dr. Carter is a sweet prof, but her lectures are painfully boring. She gives us note packets so we don't have to pay for huge textbooks, but it's hard to pay attention in class bc the notes are provided for you. 

Only 3 exams and a paper, so you have to do well on all of them to get a good grade. Take her if you want an easy A but a boring class

Loved her class so much. I find the HUMAs painfully boring, and she somehow made me WANT to come to class. Miracle worker. Also so, so understanding and hilarious. Wonderful person, wonderful teacher. 10/10 recommend.

I took Civ Arts with Munson and cried after most classes. I dropped out because I was going to fail and took Carter the next year, expecting it to be the same. It was not. Carter is so kind and thoughtful, so sweet and engaging, and she loves the material she teaches. She gives you every possible tool you need to ace the class. I love her so much.

One of the best classes I've taken at Grove City. Zero work outside of class other than studying for tests. Engaging lectures, fantastic sense of humor, clear expectations. Not sure which was more evident, her passion for the content or her desire to foster appreciation for art and music within her students. Dr. Carter is an absolute UNIT.
Fantastic professor, explains material clearly and concisely.
Dr. Shaw's class was super easy. He was very generous on points for exams and told a lot of jokes in class! He got a little sidetracked at times but I was still able to understand the material.
Dr. Shaw makes class entertaining and worthwhile.  He corny jokes every day that will make you laugh or smile.  Definitely take him if you get a chance!
Great guy, take him any chance you can get, especially for labs.

This guy can literally teach this whole course out of his head. Hes so smart. He knows the class is kinda boring so he spices it up with his cheesy jokes which are a treat bc theyre only funny bc hes the one saying it. He even let us watch price is right one day in class. hes legit

He's an outstanding professor and willing to help at all times. His tests are fair and always based on the homework or the notes. Expect to do work, especially since I took Calc II, but if you put in the effort he will notice and give you ample opportunity to succeed.

While I personally loved him as a teacher, he often struggled to get a clear, concise point across to my classmates. Often his teaching would get tangled up in math-speak, which not everyone is fluent with. Especially if you took him freshman year after taking calc I in highschool. Definitely a major nerd, which I vibe with.

I love Dr. Smith, but he's probably too mathy for those who don't learn math by understanding. 
Pros: Projects iPad. Nerdy-ly funny (N: "Thou shall not pass"). Accessible, friendly, and responsive (Mathematica) in office hours. Gives neat note packets. 
"Cons": Wkly quizzes. 4 tests. Not much curves. Attention for grades. Most love or hate...

By far the worst experience I've had thus far at this college. His notes are extremely disorganized, multiple exams had an average in the low 50's, he required us to memorize proofs we didn't fully understand, he expected us to use math jargon we were never taught, and honestly revealed that he cares very little about our overall well-being.

Calc II is supposed to be a weeder class, but somehow Dr. Smith made it harder than just that. Lectures were solid and informative, but tangents unrelated to the material came often. Homework was easy, but that just made the exams and quizzes that much harder. This class is manageable, but expect to be challenged beyond what was required in Calc I.

Cedric is the most unorganized professor I have ever taken. He does not follow a syllabus, he has exteme expectations about the amount of work a student can do and also makes up assignments on the spot. Many times, he assigns overnight projects and work. We do not know when we are being graded and when he does provide a grade he uses CHATGPT for it

Business Law - Not a fun class, no matter what professor you get. The assigned readings are horribly long. His lectures can get kind of scattered, but clear on the whole. He diluted the extreme volume of course material really well by having exams frequently. His syllabuses are notoriously flexible and assignments might be given on short notice.

Intro to Music Marketing was a really fun class. Students market a selection of independent artists in small groups throughout the semester. The sum of their efforts turns into a 45 page marketing plan and group presentation directly to the artists as a final. Very heavy workload but the course material is fantastic for Music Business Majors.

ENTR326 Intro to the Business of Music was a fantastic class. There is a high volume of material to cover and the workload is heavy but Lewis is able to convey key concepts very well. This class is an absolute gem for anybody who is a Music Business major. Lewis is also extremely accessible outside of class and gives excellent feedback.

Business Law This is a required class for business majors so there are plenty other people teaching it- just avoid this guy. Class has next to nothing to do with business. Constantly changing syllabus, you never know what he'll grade. Started class one day with "You have a 4,000 word paper due in 2 weeks." Nice person, makes sense, worst class ever

Professor English was one of my favorite professors. The class teaches what it looks like to run a startup and you get to do it hands on. Her syllabus is clear and due dates are laid out well. She also provides ample rubrics and other tools needed to be successful. I also enjoyed that she allows "coaching" so that you can improve if needed.

I took ENTR 201 with Professor English. She is a caring professor and wants your team to succeed. Your team is a big determiner of whether you'll like or hate the class. It was interesting to see how to form a startup.

Loved Prof. English. The Life Design Studio class had some pretty vulnerable topics, and I was surprised to find myself completely comfortable sharing some really personal things in the written reflections. You can tell she's a super cool and genuine person in real life; would highly recommend taking her even if you're not in entrepreneurship!

Professor English is such a fun professor!! ENTR102 is not normally online but COVID... anyway it was a great class, learned a lot. Wasn't a ton of homework, but it was squeezed into 5 weeks which was really fine. Take this class even if you don't have too! Professor English is so great!!

Was not a fan. He makes fun of you. way too much work. way too many quizzes. Never gives your grades back from papers. Forgets to put VHL homework online. One day he forgot to give us homework and gave it to us sunday night expecting us to finish it monday.

101 is largely about memorizing vocab and being able to hold basic conversations in Spanish. I highly recommend going to every language table event you can so that you feel prepared by the end of the semester for the oral exam. In class, be sure to purposeful with the time he alots for partner conversation.
Professor Killam is a good and caring professor but fails to clearly teach Spanish to English-speaking students as he mostly talks in Spanish. It never works.

Dr. Killam is a phenomenal professor. He was not afraid to freely talk about his faith and he was sure to teach the grammar parts in English so that there was no confusion. He encouraged me daily and gave me direct, personal feedback. I highly recommend taking him.

Dr. Killam is the best! Super clear expectations in class, engaging lectures, and he lays out clearly what will be on tests. Very reasonable/generous grader, his class sets you up for success if you're willing to put in the work to memorize the vocab and learn tenses. Genuinely cares about his students, one of my favorite teachers at GCC.

Dr. Bibza is not a very engaging speaker and his lectures grew repetitive and hard to follow. He never seemed satisfied with our class performance and engagement. However, he did show an openness to meet with students who reached out. The reading quizzes are easy, but the exams can be difficult as they come from the lectures.

Dr. Bibza is an awesome professor. He teaches in a simple old school way. He speaks and you have to pay close attention so that you can take good notes. Overall, classes with him are fun. He teaches the material but also forms a great connection with his students.

Dr. Bibza is one of my all time favorite professors! Yes, the class is difficult, but he just wants to help us learn. He is very caring, and always wants to help his students out. Not only is he a great professor with the information, but he also shares his wisdom about life with us. Best professor ever!

Dr. Mueller is an amazing choir director. She worked with me when I had schedule conflicts and helped me catch up with music that I missed. She is extremely knowledgable and very gifted at explaining vocal techniques. She can be hard to approach at first, but is actually very kind and willing to help. I could not imagine a better choral director.
Dr. Mueller is awesome! She's funny and engaging, making concert choir a blast even in the grind of learning challenging pieces. If you show up to every class and participate, it's an easy A.

Dr. Mueller is one of the greatest professors at Grove City and is a phenomenal musician.  She really knows her stuff and is great at conveying that knowledge to her students.  She does a good job at presenting information multiple ways if students are having trouble understanding.  Her office hours are very helpful.  She is intimidating at times.

Such a great class. Little to no homework/ reading. There is daily reading quizzes but they are such a small percent of your grade and are so easy that if you have any grasp on economics you should be able to guess and get them right. Prof was so good at explaining everything. Take good notes and try to participate in class and you'll do well.

Great class- learned a ton.
A LOT of reading though, and he quizzes on every single reading assignment.
Tests come mostly from lectures.

Dr. Ritenour is not merely an expert in his field, but a very excellent, and well-humored teacher who genuinely enjoyed teaching this class on the Foundations of Economics. He teaches with a great deal of zeal and from a solid Christian background. I hope to take many of his classes in the future.
reading quizzes are tough but one of the most fun teachers you will ever have. As long as you try you will get a good grade and you will have a great time doing it

You have to do the reading in order to pass the mini quiz at the beginning of every class, but the following lecture is just a repeat of the reading (he wrote the textbook). I found it very redundant. The material is fairly easy to grasp, and the tests are relatively easy. Dr. Ritenour is hilarious and keeps class entertaining. Not much reading.
Very cute little old guy. Super easy class, although he can be very confusing sometimes with assignments. His exams are of varying degrees of difficulty, so I never really knew what to expect.

Dr. Jenkins is the light at the end of the tunnel for BIOL 102. He gives you fill in the blank notes that are actually an accurate representation of what you need to know for the test. He also gives lots of extra credit and curves tests. He has a quirky sense of humor and he is always willing to explain things during office hours.

Dr. Jenkins is awesome!  He has a great sense of humor, is passionate about his field, explains the material clearly, and is accessible outside of class.  I would definitely recommend any of his classes or labs!

It took everything I had to stay awake every single class and I'm not someone who normally sleeps. But don't miss class! You'll fail the test even if you miss one day. Every PowerPoint slide is somehow crucial. Also get to class early because for some reason he only prints note outlines for 1/2 the class. You show up on time - no notes for you.
Monotone and the lectures are long and not exciting; however, the class is easy and he curves the tests a lot. An easy A.

The most amazing lectures ever. Had the class as an 8:00AM and if it was any other class I'd probably be falling asleep but this one was amazing. Very tough grader--you'll need to put A LOT of effort to succeed but he is definitely one of my fav professors.

Dr. Graham is one of the nicest men you will ever meet. his quizzes require you to read but they often are over surprising or interesting facts from the reading. Talk with him if you can show that you have deep understanding of the material. Highly recommend.

Dr. Graham is personable and friendly, and his class is very interesting. However, his quizzes and tests tend to be a mixed bag... some questions are layups, other questions are entirely too specific - this forces you to read the textbooks not for an understanding of the lesson/concept but to try to catch any odd bit of information for the quiz.

best professor of my college experience so far. amazing lectures. loved by all. will be difficult and you have to take quality notes on all of the readings. incredibly supportive and just a clearly nice and good person

Professor Graham is nice and seems to know what he's talking about, but you NEED to pay attention to the lectures and readings. The readings take a LONG time. His quizzes are very specific even if you do the readings.

Best professor ever! I must have done so well that he put me on the team! His monthly tests were very difficult... he liked to call them buckets. My favorite day was when I got to try out every single race at one meet. Fritzy is hilarious but don't make him angry... beware. I am retaking to see if i can do better next time!! Wish me luck!

the best time of my life ever, these are the days. he told me I had so much potential that he put me on the actual team. but then I stopped getting better and had a quarter-life crisis, going back next year we'll see what happens I guess. love you professor fritzypoo
Funny dude. Tells you the answers to his final. He is also a good advisor!
Coach Fritz is da bomb!
very good fitwell class. did learn something. good guy. put swimming on your wishlist instead of circuit training

Dr. Kriley has been my favorite professor at GCC so far. The class is entirely manageable, and he is VERY clear about what is on the quizzes/exams. If you pay attention in lecture and take good notes, you will succeed. Dr. Kriley is easily accessible and answers his emails quickly. As long as you put the hours in, you'll succeed.
I love dr kriley and no i am not a male and I do not play rugby, but I sure wish i did.

I had Dr. Kriley for Gen Chem I&II and both of those labs. He was great in every course, he typically explains concepts in a way that most people can understand.  He also provides practice tests before every test that give you a pretty good idea of what to expect.

I loved Dr. Kriley in General Chemistry I and II. I also had him for lab for both of those classes. He is good at explaining the material and answering questions (both during and outside of class). Definitely come to him if you need homework help. As long as you can do the problems on his practice tests, you should fine on the actual tests.

I was not a fan of Dr. Kriley when I first met him and I was reluctant to take Chem 2 with him. But I'm glad to be wrong. He lectures like he's sitting down and having coffee with you and is pretty funny. He's clear about what will be on tests/quizzes and gives you old exams to practice with that are like the ones you will take.

Could not recommend more if you're looking to expand your Biblical knowledge. Dr. Bibza's wisdom is incredible and has broadened my perspective of the Bible and Theology. Funny, kind, and challenging but reasonable. Legend.

Bibza is an exceptional Biblical scholar. His Hebrews course is, no doubt, a tough class. But more so than other classes, you get what you put into it. It is a Bible study for 3 credits, not a bad deal! Dry lectures, but attend class. Tests and quizzes are reasonable, definitely make sure to put good time into studying for both.

NT was much easier than OT with less heavy reading, and easier quizzes. 3 difficult exams and must study if you want to pass, NT is less focused on memorizing long lines of Kings like OT which makes class more enjoyable. Bibza is a really good professor but would suggest just taking Bib Rev if you don't want an extra class for your huma core !
Very sweet and witty professor. Class wasn't hard at all but incredibly insightful! Plenty of extra credit opportunities are offered if needed.

Dr. Bibza is an incredible professor. The class is hard, so make sure to do the reading. He often gives a full letter grade worth of extra credit to those willing to put in the work. Definitely recommend.
Dr. Ayers' class is super interesting and he gives students everything they need to know to prep for quizzes and tests. Quizzes and Exams can be tricky so make sure you study well and do the reading.
Dr. Ayers' SOCI 101 class was very enjoyable.  He uses a lot of anecdotes to explain the concepts, and I feel I gained a very good understanding of the material.

Dr. Ayers is a professor who truly loves teaching, but his ego often gets in the way. He would rather tell personal stories and talk about his own achievements than lecture about the actual information needed for exams. He is very friendly and can accommodate any student's needs. His exams are few and he is a tough grader for the essay portions.

While I didn't find his SOCI101 lecture content to be that interesting, Dr. Ayers has a very funny, out-of-pocket, and dry sense of sarcasm that made the class worthwhile. His exams are multiple choice and essay based- but they're all open note and open text. You'll finish the class having heard lots of great stories and weird random facts.

Dr. Ayers is an excellent professor who has lots of experience in the anthropology field. His lectures can be somewhat dry, but he's a very funny guy. He teaches students multiple different approaches to help them think for themselves. Quizzes and tests are open book, open note. Attendance is important, as not all topics are on the guided notes.
Professor Miller is great as always. It was great to know how to write for different mediums. I especially liked writing a movie review and doing a television script read with a team.

To clarify, I took COMM 359, not 350. Professor Miller is 1 of my favorite teachers I've had while at Grove City. She makes the subject interesting and gives good feedback. I believe I've learned things that will help me be a better writer.
I love Dr. Miller. COMM 135 was an excellent first class to take for the communications major and I would definitely recommend her! She's funny, caring, and most of all, easy to approach and helpful.
If you enjoy writing and random pop culture refs, youll love Dr. Miller. She is funny and caring and youll learn a lot!
She really cares about her students but seems to possess only surface level knowledge of the topics she is teaching.

Coach Gibson's classes were my favorite. They really prepared you for working in real life. He taught us things that we would actually apply to the Sports industry. I would take his class any day 1000%
Awesome Guy. Reasonable with grades, entertaining. Highly recommend

Professor Garhart was a great teacher. He really wanted you to learn and excel. He strove to accomplish just that. He's there to help you succeed in the actual workforce. It's a lower level class but I learned a ton from him. I would take his class again

This is a great option for one of your MECE electives senior year even though you're probably not going into the HVAC realm for work after school. Dr. Bardy did a very good job with this class and made sure students understood concepts, weren't overworked, and graded fairly. Would definitely recommend.

I like Dr. Bardy but he was rough for fall semester junior lab. I would look into taking Dixon if you can. The layout of the lab was bad. Reading complex lab handouts then getting quizzed on them, collecting data then performing analytical gymnastics to produce your lab memos. All the while Dr. Bardy was not too enthusiastic about helping you out.

Dr. Bardy is a wonderful man. He has a fantastic sense of humor which made lectures entertaining but he moves pretty quick through the material. Tests are worth a majority of your grade but he is a decently fair grader.  Ultimately Dr. Bardy if one of the few MECE or Grove City professors in general who actually wants their students to succeed.

Let's be honest. If your a Mechanical engineer you have to take this course. But, Bardy is a really good teacher and he does a great job of giving just enough example problems in class to not be overwelming. He actually cares about you learning the material too.
Dr. Bardy is the man.  Tough homeworks, but they make you learn the material.  Great guy.

FEA was really taught in 2 sections: theory and software. All the theory made no sense at all and seemed like a general waste of time. However, learning ANSYS and Creo Simulate was super valuable so I would recommend taking the class but don't worry too much about the theory portion.
The capstone class is required as a MECE/EE so you're stuck with him for now. Get ready to learn a lot about printers. Bring a coffee/snack and you'll be fine.

Puts random test questions on test that are not like homework or examples from class.  Do NOT take MECE 408 Mechanical Vibrations.  Nice guy, but when you've got 3 test problems and you have no clue how to do 1 of them, it becomes a problem.
I learned so much in engineering design. Class was easy nevertheless. very good man who cares abput students and the subjects he teaches

Very friendly, but this man has no idea how to teach upper level classes. I've learned nothing. We spend more time correcting him than he does teaching. He copies examples from the book and can't explain them. His tests are so random you wont know what to expect - most of them are entirely too long. The "fact of the matter" is avoid him if you can.

Dr. Bright's ethics class is a great option to help you fill out your MECE electives senior year. It was a Tuesday night class that only met the first 6 weeks of the semester and was an easy A for a 1 credit class.

Introduction to Engineering with Dr. Bright was one of my favorite courses I took that semester. It is a project HEAVY course so make sure to pick a good group. That being said, the Robotic Arm Project was a blast, he gave engaging and informative lectures (his sense of humor is fantastic), and was very reasonable about homework and exams.

Dr. Bright understands and conveys the material well. He is beloved even by people who didn't do well in his classes. His humor is dry, but his lectures are engaging and helpful. He grades homework, projects, and exams fairly. Digital Logic Design is a required class for electrical engineers, but related majors would do well to take it if possible.

Dr. Bright is a great professor. He is intelligent, has a great sense of humor, and has the ability to make an otherwise boring subject interesting. His tests aren't easy, but you'll find that he is extremely helpful during office hours and his grading system is fair.

Dr. Bright is great. Lots of fun, teaches well, interesting classes, very helpful, very smart. Tests and homework are challenging and fun. Gives grades slightly higher than the rest of the EE dept. Technical Management is one of the best classes at GCC.
no gyatt

beyond easy. This course has all the exams online, the material is easy to grasp, and he often just rambles on about random current event stuff (which is interesting). If you are looking for an easy class - this is your guy. And let me just say, I am not good at school in any way. I would not rate professor Carson as being easy if he wasn't.

Enjoy semi-retirement Dr. Carson. For those taking his MNGT 103 class going forward, just make sure to actually read the textbook for reading quizzes and pay attention in class. The class is super easy if you do both of the above consistently.

Carson has a great class overall. Quizzes are frequent but aren't too difficult as long as you do the reading and take good notes. Always do the review on Connect, it helps so much. His tests are not that difficult either, but his lectures are kind of boring and not really related to the textbook material. Overall it's not a difficult class.

Dr. Carson is absolutely amazing. His quizzes aren't hard, though there are a lot of them, and he is very open to talking about anything. He genuinely cares about each of his students, and you could go to him and talk about anything you need to- even if it's not course related.

By far the most engaging and interesting business professor I have taken. I would 100% recommend taking his course. He is passionate about his subject and very personable. The one downside is that he is a tough grader, but get over it, just do your work

Very tough teacher. Feedback was not helpful. Finds a way to take points off of the simple questions. It is important to understand his style. Figure out how he likes things to be answered. Open ended questions killed me on the exams, and he grades very harsh on the homework. Definitely my least favorite business class so far.

Prof. Butler is an expert in global business and he does an excellent job of teaching the course content. He is very engaging and will memorize your name and ask questions so make sure you do the readings/are paying attention in class. He is a super harsh grader and is unafraid to rock you on exams, homework, and especially country in focus.

I LOVE Dr. Huebert!!!
I took her Civ Arts class during May intersession. 
She is a hilarious professor with super easy tests and extra credit. Definitely would take again! 
SHE IS JUST AMAZING I LOVE HER

Unless you like a 75-minute lecture where the professor just rambles on about pictures on a slide show with no bullet points, this class is not for you. STEAR CLEAR of CIVARTS with Dr. Huebert. The only 5 grades in the class are tests and one essay paper. She prepares us horribly for tests, and the study guides are extremely vague. Terribly boring.

Dr. Huebert is a very straightforward professor. She will lecture a lot and give you tests directly based off of the material learned in class. If you pay attention to the lectures, it's easy to do well which is why I liked it. She grades papers and tests fairly

Dr. Hubert is terrible with technology, unclear on grading and a horrible communicator. She does not explain things well and does not care about whether students succeed. If you want to enjoy learning, do not take this class!

Dr. Huebert is a wonderful professor! She will work with you on due dates or anything-just ask! The reading and paper is light and fun. Her lectures come straight from the heart. She is kind-hearted and soft spoken, so it is really important to stay focused and listen to the reflective things she has to say throughout class.

Dr. Owen is the worst proffessor I've ever had. Not only can he not effectively communicate his lectures, he tests on the most abstract ideas within the class. He is by far the worst professor on this campus and I would like to see him excused immediately. Godspeed to anyone who takes any single one of his courses.

What a great guy! The way he teaches intro to finance is both instructive and also easy to comprehend. I found the tests and homework all relatively easy and had a 96% before the final after putting in little effort. Between the easy class load and Owen's personality, I'm looking forward to doing another class with him soon.

Owen is a great guy overall. He is extremely intelligent but has difficulty lecturing sometimes as English is not his native language. His courses are relatively easy if you dedicate time towards doing the homework and studying. What he says will be on the exam will in fact be on the exam and he doesn't try to ever confuse you. Definitely take him.

I can't say Dr. Wolfe is a bad professor, I'm just not cut out to be a programmer. However, the class I took is geared towards those who have a background in coding, and I didn't fit that criteria. Wolfe moved things pretty fast at times. He's a great guy though and will help you if you ask. Go to his office hours if you need help.

He is always trying to help students, but is very candid and straight to the point during his lectures, one of the only profs where i felt i had learned something during class. He even helps students with homework for other classes. Invites lots of students over to his house during finals week for good food and to hang out, great guy, real g.

Dr. Wolfe is one of the most fun, personally motivating professors I've ever had. He makes class time a really exciting time and encourages goofing off. Dr. Wolfe cares about work you've done - I had a personal project that he started using because he thought it was cool.
Dr. Wolfe is a lovely professor. He's extremely smart, but also very approachable and friendly.

Dr. Wolfe is a good lecturer and a funny guy. He would sometimes joke with us before class and always came across as a really upstanding person. His tests and projects are pretty standard but he can be very strict in his grading. One day late for a project can get you a 0.

Dr. Dickinson is a wonderful professor for Intro to Computer Science. The lectures were fun and during class there was usually some interesting activity. The class was also easy since it was an intro class, but studying for quizzes and tests was made harder since there was no study guide or practice questions.

I took this course as an elective (I'm a Biochemistry major), and it was a great choice. It turns out that coding does not come naturally to me, but Professor Dickinson was able to give me a lot of help and I ended up doing very well in the class. This is a great course for anyone inerested in learning more about computer science.

Dr Dickenson is an awesome professor!! He's one of my favorite profs in the CS department and I've had him for multiple classes. He teaches concepts well and gives clear examples, making assignments possible. He's helpful in office hours too. He genuinely enjoys the things he teaches and teaches them well. I highly recommend taking his classes!!!

Mr. Dickinson did a great job teaching Introduction to Computer Science. As a non-computer science major, this turned out to be a great elective. I learned I am definitely not a natural at coding, but Mr. Dickinson helped me every step of the way. Overall, the class has a light workload.

Professor Dickinson is a great professor who is very knowledgeable about computer science. Dickinson loves what he teaches and you can tell throughout the class. I wish he presented more opportunities to code in-class instead of class being heavily lecture-based, but Dickinson makes lectures fairly interesting. Great professor for COMP 220.

Yes, organic chemistry is hard. However, Dr. Homan does a fantastic job of teaching the material and answering questions. I wouldn't want to take organic chemistry with anyone else. He provides two practice tests before each test that are very similar to the actual test. He is also a really kind guy. Study hard and ask questions and you'll do fine.

While ochem is a pretty difficult subject to grasp, Dr. Homan makes it understandable. He is really good about answering any questions you have. Gives you old tests and answer keys to practice with so you know what to expect on the exams. He's really good for lab too! He isn't super picky and gets you out before 3 hours.

THE hardest class I have ever taken but Dr. Homan makes it worth it. Genuinely wants you to learn the material and will help you in any way to make sure you succeed. Very approachable and excited to teach the material, which makes a hard class much more bearable. Love Dr. Homan!

Dr Homan is really great for ochem. He lectures with chalkboard so taking notes in class is easy. He also gives really good practice exams before each test which are very helpful. Dr Homan is always willing to answer questions and really wants students to suceed.

Dr. Homan is an excellent teacher. He knows that the material of his classes is difficult but he is always willing to help in office hours and will often give you an entire lecture outside of class. The lab for this class is also very interesting and applicable to everyday life.

If you have to take BIOL 102, good luck. (Jenkins will save you at the end). As for Dudt's portion, his lectures are confusing, his slides are all over the place, and his tests require you to have memorized everything he said in class (if you manage to figure what he said). Just alot plenty of time for studying for quizzes and tests.

I can honestly say I could not understand half of what he was saying up until he was gone.  Questions on the test have almost nothing to do with the lecture; definitely look at the text book even though he says you don't.

Dr. Dudt is a great man, he has a lot of knowledge and interesting stories, but he is a bad professor. His lectures are full of anecdotes and rabbit trails but you never know if they are going to be tested or not! It is very hard to study because he provides no study material and his lectures are so unstructured

Incredibly boring and hard to follow lectures. Only reason why I did somewhat decent was because of the master notes, the lab grade, and the fact that Jenkins takes over halfway through the class. When I first met Dudt I thought his class was going to be great because he seems so passionate but I was very wrong. Test average is a C after the curve

I really enjoyed Dr. Dudt's BIOL 102 class. His lectures cover a lot of material, but they are great if you're interested in evolutionary theory, microorganisms, and plants. I may be a bit of a nerd, but this class has been the best I've taken yet at GCC. Be aware that the tests are very difficult--tough multiple choice, plus an essay component.

Dr. Fair is a nice guy. Introduces new developments in the world of heat transfer at the start of every class plus fun to talk to outside of class. Heat transfer was a pretty complex class but he taught it well. However, he is a pretty ruthless grader and assigns an absurd amount of homework.
I didn't understand Professor Fair's lectures most of the time which made me lost when I did the homework.

Mechanics was also taught by Dr. Clauss so I'm not sure how much she influenced the class. Either way, it had an absurd amount of homework (usually about 10 problems that took an hour each) and only 1 was graded (you don't know beforehand) so good luck with that. However, tests were pretty reasonable and there were practice exams which helped a lot

Material Science with Dr. Fair was a tough class, but the content was interesting and Dr. Fair did an excellent job delivering it. Homework and exams make up your entire grade in this class so make sure to be able to do problems he does in class and on the homework to prepare for the tests. Focus a little bit on the vocabulary but mostly problems.

By far one of the most sound academic professors in the Engineering program. He has the best notes and flow in teaching. Engineering is not supposed to be easy so work is always required for his classes but, especially as you get to more upper level classes he is more flexible.
I LOVED marching and concert band. I can't wait to get started again next year. Dr. Erb is simply the best!

Dr. Erb is the most amazing band director I have ever worked with. He cares deeply for the band and his students and treats everyone fairly and equally. I learned so much about music from him, and he sets a great example of what a Christian professor should be. I will be taking band again next fall for sure.

Jazz History with Dr. Erb was a blast. It's refreshing to have a professor be really passionate about what they are teaching. I took away so much from his class and learning about big jazzers and their stories, and I'm super inspired to keep learning about this. Thanks Dr. Erb!!

Dr. Erb is amazing. In the two years that I have had band and music ed. courses with him, I have grown to respect and admire him so much.
