He was so understanding and caring when I had a variety of mental and physical health issues during the band semester, and was always available to talk. Dr. Erb is a fantastic example of a Christian professor.
SUCH A GOOD TIME JUST DO BAND PLS

Professor Jackson was amazing! She is caring and truly wants you to suceed. She's been my favorite professor at Grove City, I doubt there's another like her. I learned so much from this class, I couldn't recommend her highly enough!

Professor Jackson is super kind and caring. She takes her job very seriously and really wants her students to succeed. What I appreciate most about Mrs. Jackson is that she's always available to answer questions and to give feedback on assignments. She's the best professor I've had so far, and I'll be hard pressed to find another teacher like her.
Professor Jackson was kind, funny, and always willing to help! The book was interesting and the assignments were simple but time consuming.

I was a little nervous when I found out she was a new teacher at Grove City but I thought she was a great teacher. She responded quickly to all messages and left helpful feedback on all of my rough drafts. I dont usually like writing but I really enjoyed her class.

This class is boring and there is a lot of reading, but she is one of the better professors to take. You do have to attend and pay attention but if you do, you shouldn't have an issue with passing this class. She is nice, just dry. It is important to do all the readings, but compared to others she is one of the better professors to take.

Honestly you can't go wrong when taking classes with Rine. Her lectures can be boring but the homework isn't bad at all and she is a very easy grader, which cannot be emphasized enough. Overall she is very kind and will help you do well in class. If you ever have the option to take a class with Rine then DO IT.

While Dr. Rine's written grading criteria can be confusing, she is more than happy to meet outside of class and explain what she wants and give feedback. Chances are, if you are respectful, can take criticism and do the work, you will be fine in her class.
A lot harder than the rest of the other classes. More work and a hard grader.

After hearing nightmarish stories from my friends about other writing professors, I think I lucked out by having Rine. Spends a lot of class time discussing helpful techniques. I would say she assigns a fair amount of papers for a writing class, but absolutely nothing overboard. Very reasonable, but can be picky when it comes to grading.

He is one of the better professors at Grove City. The lectures are enjoyable, he grades fair, and he tries to limit the amount of work you have outside of class. His class also seems to be one of the more useful classes that you will actually use.

Dr. Chapman is such a fun professor. Intro to Teaching is one of my favorite classes. However, the lecture days were long and the few times he did flip scheduling was annoying. Either way, I loved his class and would take him again.
Dr. Chapman is a fantastic professor. He loves his students and he really wants them to understand how to teach better. I would highly recommend his classes to anyone.

Dr. Chapman is fantastic! He starts ed majors off on a good foot by having him. She gives hardly any homework and the group work isn't too bad. You can really tell he loves his job and loves helping future educators.

Dr. Chapman is one of THE best ed profs on campus (you honestly can't go wrong with any of them, though). He is so kind and funny and doesn't really assign much homework in this class. His office is always open, tests are easy if you pay attention in class, and he cares about his students. I would take his class again in a heartbeat.
The class was very clear about grading, however professor Vitaly was inaccessible, no way to reach him (Email or Blackboard), somewhat tough on the grading but very clear grading criteria.
Do not take this class. This prof does not even care whether a students put effort or not. He is very disrespectful and would not mind assigning an F to a student.

So dry and boring, not a natural teacher. His lectures were just reading off the slides, and his explanations were difficult to follow. He was funny, and offered a lot of extra credit and a nice curve, but the class was just not enjoyable. I've heard in comparison to other finance profs, he is better, but good luck focusing in class. Very little HW

The guy is flat out terrible. He fails to keep you engaged during class and bores you out of your mind. The only thing I learned from him is that he likes his smoke parties. He also tests you on a bunch of concepts that he never taught you in class. So yeah, Dr. Grimm, lousy prof. Avoid him if you can.

Easy to get an A. One of the worst "teachers" I've ever had in my life. The man is lecturing style is absolutely horrible and he teaches literally nothing. Don't even bother showing up to his class - complete waste of your time.

His lectures are so engaging solely because of how hilarious he is. It's a pretty easy A, but I still learned a lot. Unlike some of the other finance professors, he teaches and allows students to use a financial calculator, which is practical for a career in the field. He is definitely the best choice for the course.
Good dude

My favorite class I have ever taken, and it was easy. The content was so interesting and she made class very engaging. I loved hearing the stories from her personal experiences with different cultures and as a missionary. Truly looked forward to class everyday. Assignments were lengthy, but about interesting concepts so they were actually enjoyable

Professor Barrios has lived in many cultures, and teaches about other cultures well. Her class was very eye-opening, and taught me a lot about other cultures and other perspectives. There are a few papers in the class, but that was not a huge deal for me. Some of the papers were pretty fun to write. An A is pretty easy if you put in  some effort.

Professor Barrios is amazing! Her lectures are interesting and the in-class activities are fun. The assignment are relevant to what you are learning and very manageable. I look forward to going to her classes everyday. She cares a lot about her students and is respected by them. 

11/10. Take her classes if you get the chance

I absolutely adore her classes. I've taken three different classes of hers so far, and I plan to take more in the future. She's an amazing person and so quick to help. She clearly loves her students and the Lord, and is just a joy to be around. I HIGHLY recommend her classes. She is a super easy grader and doesn't give a lot of homework.

Professor Barrios is incredible. She is funny, a great listener, cares about more than her students grades, and genuinley wants to hear what we have to say. She is so encouraging too! There is nothing negative to say about her.

Dr. Mohr is a sweet man. Personable guy and actually cares about student success. Very patient teaching electrical engineering to a room full of sweaty meces. Lab was a great hands on learning experience plus he always lets you out on time. Lectures were a bit dry and the content got a bit above my head towards the end of the semester. Solid class.

Dr. Mohr is the most overrated professor in GCC's engineering department. He's a nice person, but his classes are seriously hard to succeed in if you're not a natural genius. His lectures often barely scratch the surface of what you'll actually be tested on. Grading feedback on homework is of zero help and it never is similar to tests. Don't take!

Dr. Mohr is an exceptional professor who explains the material well. However, Linear Circuits I is the hardest class I've ever taken. The average on the first exam was a 66% and he doesn't curve. Only one of around 20 students received an A. He definitely uses ELEE 201 to weed weaker students out of the electrical engineering major.

Excellent teacher who makes difficult concepts comprehensible. There is an abundance of homework, but it is clear what he wants. The exams are similar difficulty to the homework so if you learn the material there shouldn't be any surprises.

Dr. Mohr is an amazing professor. He knows his material incredibly well and he's good at teaching it. He goes out of his way to help you in his office, and many times stayed late to help me. One of my favorite profs. Very, very helpful. Course material is difficult, but he's good at teaching it. Great choice.

Grades took weeks to be returned. Dr. Zhang "teaches" relational algebra in one class and then assumes you know how to do everything. twice something the professor said wouldn't be on a test ended up on the test. The professor still hasn't moved to Intellij and still uses eclipse but did not teach how to use the IDE.

Now finally wrapping up Intro to LabView, he definitely deserves a good rating. Lectures were once a week, so most times you had to learn outside the class. Overall, work is average and doable, Projects are fairly straight forward at times, and he makes himself accessible for all questions. Would take again and highly recommend him for any class.

Dr. Rumbaugh is generally well-liked by his students. He is a new professor and it sometimes shows in his organization, but he generally presents the material simply and clearly. He is uniquely gifted in lab work, he grades assignments fairly, and he is willing to answer questions both during class and his office hours. Take his classes if you can!

Dr. Hasper is very kind and clearly loves music. He will play Taylor Swift and John Legend in class and compares Beethoven to Lizzo. However, he is quiet and doesn't really explain super well, and looks like he has stage fright teaching. However, he is a great person and is ready to help if you need it.
Music theory is easy no matter who teaches it. Hasper is just real awkward.

Lets be clear - Dr. Christy Crute is literally my favorite teacher in this entire school. She is not what the awful responses below say about her. She goes literally above and beyond to help students. Her course is exceptionally fair and she is very talented at what she does. I for one am not a good exam taker AT ALL, but she helped me do well.

Dr. Crute doesn't deserve most of the hate she gets on this website. She is willing to spend as much time as possible outside of class to make you understand the topic. The class is a lot of work, but the hw/quizzes set you up for success on the exams. She is understanding and will adjust the class as needed if you talk to her. Overall, good prof

I had Dr. Crute for a graduate level class. Maybe those who want to be coddled like children view things differently but I found her to be a tremendous. Dr. Crute stressed not only the book material but also real-world applications. However, I can see her being difficult for those who crave structure or want to be spoon-fed lessons.
This was the worst experience I have ever had with a professor. She never explained anything properly and came across as crazy, making everyone uncomfortable.
I hated showing up to an 8 AM class for nothing. Huge waste of my time and money for a required class. The students literally deserve an apology for this disorganized disaster professor.

look, not many people want to take Spanish. That includes me. But I took Forteza because what I heard is right - she's a bit easier than most Spanish GCC professors. The questions on exams or homework can be usually found online, and she's a very nice lady. however, the homework is annoyingly frequent.

I loved Forteza in SPAN 301, but this past class was so aggravating . She was unorganized , unclear, and hard to reach. I liked her as a person, and as I said, thought she was terrific in other classes from past semesters. However, this class is why I dropped my Spanish minor.

Jumped into class with VERY little Spanish experience years ago in HS, had no clue what I was doing. Class almost entirely in Spanish which is difficult & terrifying, but she does a great job of explaining things/making sure you understand her. She's ALWAYS willing to help you during office hours and is a very fair grader. Hard but learned a lot.

I wish I could choose more tags to show just how awesome Dr. Buxton is. His class is very focused on being an inspiring learning environment that will foster your passion for the topic. He doesn't call on random people, but his classes are very interactive so they fly by. The more you participate, the more you will get out of his classes.
Was a great professor! Lots of homework but felt like your grade reflected how hard you worked. A very understanding professor, willing to work with you to help you succeed.
Professor Wolinski's lab sessions had only a few interesting science demonstrations but the rest was just in-class work that was boring to get through.

I am Wolinski's biggest fan. He is by far the funniest guy on staff here at Grove. Not everyone is the biggest fan of his teaching style, but I enjoy it. Some of my favorite experiences with faculty in my time here at GCC are just vibing in his office with friends, goofing off with him like a bunch of kids. I can't recommend this guy enough.

Dr. Wolinski is great. His teaching style isn't for everyone, but it worked great for me. I find myself going to his office hours even when I no longer have him as a professor just to talk about life and engineering things. Dr. Wolinski is a great professor and I highly recommend.

Dr. Wolinski is probably one of my favorite professors. He is super easy to talk to and has an underlying dry sense of humor which is always nice. His class can be difficult, but he is always there to help. I found myself just talking to him about random engineering things in office hours even when I didn't need help. He's a really great professor.

You can tell he is definitely passionate about the topics he teaches. He teaches multiple classes so it sometimes feels like he rushes through material or is busy (which I'm sure he is). He is helpful during office hours and the class is doable as long as you are willing to put in extra work. Physics isn't supposed to be a cake walk after all.
I took Professor Hammond online over intersession. He had great lectures and tutorials that interested me in design, though it sometimes took him a while to answer questions.

Amazing lectures that keep your attention and make you love design, but HORRIBLE at keeping deadlines. Does not do what he says he will do by a certain date; we got multiple de facto breaks (not that I'm complaining about the breaks, it's just I would have appreciated more communication) and everything just felt really confusing and unclear.

Ryan is an amazing professor who cares about all his students and makes them feel seen and heard. He has a lot of knowledge to share, despite being a younger prof. Ive taken three classes with him and look forward to taking more, and I have learned so much from every class. You need to participate, but he makes it easy to do so!

Ryan is a great guy who brings his sense of humor and professional experiences to each class. He's very down to earth and laid back, and his assignments are very clear and straightforward. Because he's a younger guy, I think he sometimes feels a little uncomfortable teaching people relatively close to his age, but he does a great job.

Ryan really cares about the actual student is is very understanding about every circumstance. He went here and understands how tough the school can be. His class was 100 percent worth it and I will look for classes with him teaching in the future.

Very difficult and lots of work but I learned A LOT! I also got amazing out of class experiences with fields and being actively involved with students. It was amazing experience and you learn a lot from real life experience. I probably learned the most in this class compared to my others.

Mrs. Martin is the best ed professor I have taken here. She is tough, and expects a lot, but she lives and breathes what she teaches everyday. I learned a lot from her lectures and then fields and other activities that she assigns. It's very hands on and she is honest and tells you the truth. She prepares you for a future in Special Education!
Collin Messer is the reason I get up in the morning

I took American Lit II with Messer, and it is by far the worst experience I've ever had in a literature course. He takes everything VERY seriously and his style is heavy and even oppressive. Also has unreasonably high expectations and grades harshly. Half your grade is spitting back out lecture material rather than forming your own literary ideas.

everyone loves messer & i admit he is a nice man who obviously cares about his students. BUT as an english major i do not enjoy class w him. he is so serious about everything pertaining to school work. he places ur integrity & very character as a human being on the line if u aren't able to do 100% of the 60 pages of reading he assigns each night.

Really a fantastic professor--you will learn so much in his classes. Reading quizzes can be challenging but are definitely doable as long as you are attentive in your reading. Midterm and final are fine as long as you study and take thorough notes in class. Do NOT procrastinate on the paper; Dr. Messer is happy to help you with it if you ask him.

Dr. Messer is AMAZING. He only teaches one section of Writing, so get it if you can! As freshmen, his essays are amazing because they're applicable to our lives and help in trying to figure ourselves out as students and scholars. He prays/reminds us of our faith every class, which is easily forgotten in the midst of busy college life.

This professor developed a habit of responding rudely and sometimes aggressively with any questions I had toward her during her online lecturing. She expected us to be absolute experts, but her teaching style is lack luster at best.

Dr. Al Moakar is an excellent professor. Her lectures are creative and helpful, and she's always willing to repeatedly address concepts about which students are confused. She works to ensure students are comfortable in her classroom, even allowing them to listen to music during exams. Her exams are fair but very challenging to complete.
Mandatory to take her if you're in CS. She can definitely be kind but I find her unpleasant to be around. Her teaching style is not very good in my opinion and her lectures are extremely boring.
Lectures are boring and mostly unhelpful but attendance is required.
Best Professor I have had.

I had Professor Guevara for CHEM 113 (O) and she was average. It was cool doing some of the experiments in the lab but there was a lot I didn't understand completely. The labs were in pairs so as long as you and your partner know what you're doing, you should be fine.

Dr. Guevara is definitely better than what people tell you. Her gen chem class has lots of busy work but the assignments are an easy 100% that really boost your grade and therefore you hardly have to worry about the midterm and final (the ONLY exams). She genuinely listens to complaints and changes what she is doing to make the class better.

The lectures clearly outline the course content and almost always relate to the end of chapter quizzes. Do not let the midterm final setup of this class kill you. If you make sure to understand each unit as you go, you should do well in this course.

Professor Guevera is very by the book. The course is right out in front of you and if you put in the work you will succeed. She is very nice and takes the time to explain things and go through many examples. I would definitely take her class again.

Dr. Guevara is super kind and always willing to help answer any questions or problems! Her lectures are sometimes hard to grasp but filling in the gaps with YT videos helps. She's great to have for lab and definitely wants her students to succeed! (Freshman 111 students, make sure you do all the HW since it's such a large part of the grade!)

As an education major who was not familiar with music/arts, this was one of my favorite courses. Dr. Paparone is WONDERFUL! She understood that we were not music majors but made our experience fun and interesting. The midterm and final are fair & not bad. Please participate though, even if you don't enjoy singing, you will love this!

Dr. Paparone is the sweetest professor who will always be accessible for students whether it is for a homework question or a much needed cry on her couch. She grades somewhat on the easier side and expects you to participate in class.
Awesome professor! Will help with everything and anything. Go to her if you have problems; she'll help you out. :) Get to know her one on one also.

LOVE Mrs. Munson! I am not the best at math but this class was awesome. We learned ways to teach math and the best possible ways to make students successful. The projects in this class were so interesting and creativity is what she loves! Participate and be engaged & you will be fine!

You will learn so much in your time in the EEC. Mrs. Munson gives you ample opportunities to connect with the students and apply what you're learning in your Education classes. She's kind, compassionate, and hilarious - her feedback is fantastic. You'll leave her EEC feeling very confident in where you stand in Education.
Tricia is so kind and good at what she does!

Love Tricia! She cares about you as a student beyond your grade. If you forgot to print the (rare) assignmet, shell say this is a problem that is easy to fix. Even printed it for me just to put a  on it. I learned so many new things about public speaking. She wants you to succeed too!!

I took this class as an elective and it ended up being my favorite course of the semester. Tricia makes the class really fun and she truly cares about every student in the classroom. She really helped me become a better speaker and I now have more confidence in myself when speaking. If you need this course or have an open elective, take her class.
Professor Pritchard was the most caring professor I had. I very much enjoyed her class and would take it again if I could.

Unfortunately, I just did not enjoy or learn anything in Prof. Pritchard's class. Her personality is very unique, so if you do not like it the class becomes very unfun. On top of that, the lectures explained little more than what to do on the next assignment, all of which I could have found through Google. Easy A but a waste of time.
Touch grader! Too many exams, lots of homework, test heavy. Don't take him.
a very TOUGH GRADER professor. It seems like he doesn't care about the student and doesn't want you to get good grades.
No Comments
Please refer to Saint Vincent College's William Birmingham as they are the same person.

Dr. Birmingham is an all around great guy and great professor (and he really loves games). He truly wants his students to succeed and is willing to help in any way.  He's a very easy grader, and recognizes the hard work you put into your programs. Always willing to talk to you about anything and give advice. He's like a dad to everyone.
Dr. Birmingham is a great guy. Material in class was very informative. Best part about him though is his helpfulness outside of class. Always seemed genuinely interested in me. Very approachable.

Birmingham's classes (at least comp 222) aren't that hard. I got 100's on all the projects, because he basically gives you all of the code for them in advance. You just have to piece them together. He always seems a little unprepared for class whick is a little annoying. If you know his study guides for tests, they won't be bad at all.
very poor professor.  he is unclear on his assignments, and imposes arbitrary guidelines which are not helpful to the understanding of the material.  avoid if at all possible

Dr. Schaefer was absolutely wonderful. He tells you everything to know for the quizzes and tests ahead of time (including the midterm and final exams), so he hands you all you need, all you need to do is put in the time to do the work. I found myself zoning out of his lectures a few times, but other than that it was great. Puts comics on his PPTs.

Great course and a great professor! Dr. Schaefer is a real goofy and fun guy and he truly cares about his students so much. He makes sure that papers and exams/quizzes are easy so that students can focus on gaining a deeper unerstadning of topics rather than simple memorization of correct answers. RELI261 is the perfect mix of fun and enlightening!

He is a very caring professor that prepares you for both tests. Class is very boring and lecture heavy but he tries to throw fun parts in. The midterm and final were rough but he holds a review session the night before and he basically gives you the test questions.
so boring and i never went to class.
Passionate and profoundly knowledgeable about Scripture. Lectures can be hard to follow sometimes due to wandering slightly off topic, but still an great professor

I'm not sure what I loved more about Dr. Harmon's class, the actual language or her. She is very clear when answering questions in class, and speaks only in French nearly 100% of the time which is challenging but good. She has an appreciation for culture too, and is not afraid to go on rabbit trails about French facts and random vocab if asked.

Dr. Harmon is an incredivle professor. She may seem intimidating at first but she is really nice and helpful and surper funny too. Class is almost always spoken all in French to get the full imersion experience but you can always go to her in office hours to go over stuff in English. She will definetly make time for you.

I absolutely loved this class. It can be tough but if you apply yourself and keep up with the homework everything makes sense. She is very animated and makes class fun and understandable despite being entirely in french. I always felt comfortable talking with her and she makes you feel very valued. One of my favorite professors!
So nice and her intro classes are a breeze.

Dr. Harmon is one of my favorite professors! She's really fun and does a good job at helping you understand what she's saying through facial expressions and hand motions (the entire class is taught in French). You have to put in the effort outside of class if you want to remember all the material since she speeds through the book super fast.

If you want to get yelled at to speak up, fail a bunch of quizzes even after reading, and fail a paper then Dr. Brown is the professor for you. Be prepared for the most stuck up and all around rude professor you can find! :)
I had not heard great things about Professor Brown before this class and now I understand why. If you can avoid taking her, please do.
Painful

Dr. Brown expects excellence and scholarly thinking and she makes no bones about it if students fail to measure up. Her brand of teaching definitely involves some tough love but if you suck it up, set aside your ego, and listen to what she is trying to tell you she will turn you into a better scholar and a better English major. Best professor ever.

In all honesty, Brit Lit will drain the life from you. However, it is important and necessary as the essential fundamentals  of literature. Dr. Brown is very tough, but as she should be. She wants you to be the best, buts she will not let you off easy.  She will help you if you go to her and ASK for it, but you have to do the work. Learn from her.

If you still want to like programming when you're done with this class, don't take Valentine. I showed up eager to learn code every day and as the semester went on got more and more frustrated with his unclear instructions for programs. Also Exams are weighted much, much more than the programs and zybooks which take hours upon hours to complete.

So the ratings I've seen on here show not a lack of ability or competence on Dr. V's end, but a total lack of desire or drive to actually work for anything from the reviewers. Many reviewers apparently a) expected to be able to program immediately and b) wanted A-grades to be handed to them. He's by no means perfect, but he is not at all terrible.

Valentine is just a weird being, and his existence shows how creative God is. He loves Jesus, and preached in class about how we should judge others by the same way we are being judged. He only reads off the slides and phrases test/quiz questions in creative ways to make sure we do not know the answers. God only brought him here to test my patience

Dr Valentine is the the single most incompetent professor Ive had in my life. He never returned multiple projects. He said he gave them to another prof to give back, but that other prof was completely unaware and surprised when students went and asked for their project. His tests are hard and specific, and he is a very harsh grader. An awful prof.

Dr. Valentine is an engaging lecturer with clear expectations for his class: be on time and do well. Valentine tends to get a little overexcited, which can feel intimidating to some students. His assignments are appropriately challenging for an introductory class; as someone with no programming background, I learned a great deal in his class.

Mrs. Marsch was so disorganized and her lectures are so boring. People are falling asleep left and right in this class. She will ask for one thing and when you get that assignment back she said she wanted something completely different. Even though you did exactly what she asked for. I would not take any class with her again. 10/10 not recommended

Mrs. Marsch was a very easy professor, but I've never met anyone more disorganized, disrespecful, and boring at the same time. Very rude to students when questions were asked, hours of busy work every week, the most boring lectures I've ever sat through. Safe to say she was the worst professor I had taken in my first two years of college.

Mrs. Marsch has difficulty engaging the class and spends a majority of time reviewing simple homework assignments. The work load is mostly busy work and the class is a high school level course at best. She'll give you a higher grade if you make it clear that you're putting in effort. Asking questions outside of class will make things more clear.
New teacher, wasn't the best. There was a ton of busy work, things like responding to essay readings on forms and doing grammar quizzes.

Coach Dreves does a great job teaching intro to exercise science. He's there to help you and doesn't want to see you fail. He understands that not everyone might be and exercise science major and that it's and entry level class. If you need an elective class I would highly recommend taking EXER 101 with Dreves.
He is an amazing professor. He has been my favorite professor at Grove City. He is so fun and he is very caring about you.

Coach Dreves is awesome! His lectures are interesting and gives out powerpoint sheets to follow along during the class. Half of the class grade is based off of homework, but expect to work on each assignment for at least 45 mins... so do it! 2 exams the whole semester, both were easy. Study for them and you will do great. Fun and interesting class

Dr. Stone is a very nice person but when it comes to teaching she is very scattered and can be very confusing. There is some of room to improve your grade with homework if you don't do well on the exams. Exams are very heavy on multiple choice.

Dr. Stone is great! Do all the homework because it's a big part of your grade. Come prepared to class and be ready to participate will the answers to your homework. She cares about teaching, but she is understands if accounting isn't really your thing. Such a sweet professor in class and outside class time. I definitely would take her again!
Doesn't explain well, can't teach, and is an adjunct professor who is self-admittedly "so busy".
a long time ago I took Bell and was not the best class ever but I have used what I learned A LOT
Good class + worth taking

I just rated my top 3 and bottom 3 college teachers (I finished some years ago). Bell was one of my best 3 teachers. He went well out of his way to spend time with me and even attended events class members were in. Aside from that, he knows his stuff and was refreshing break every day. Oh, he keeps office hours too SHOCKING
I was not looking forward to this class but turned out to be one that I have used a lot in my job
Well worth it one of the few classes I still use today

WOW- my favorite class I have taken at Grove City College. Dr. Cost is truly a phenomenal teacher. He kept things light and we had a good time. Dr. Cost fosters a "Dave and Busters" like environment where we all are just hanging out, having apps, and learning a ton about the founding fathers. I have recommended this class to everyone I know.
Jay Cost--what a man. It's hard to put words to a man who encompasses so much. One of the best professors at this college.

The class I took Dr. Cost for was Political Writing. The class, obviously, required a lot of writing), but it was worth it! Dr. Cost is incredibly knowledgable in the area of history and writing in politics. His feedback was very helpful in improving my own and the class's quality of work. Maybe most importantly, Dr. Cost is very funny!

One of the worst professors at GCC. Self-righteous, abrasive, and acts as if she's better than/smarter than everyone else around her (she is not). Class wasn't bad, but having to be in the same room as her was. Also, someone teaching at GCC should know that women cannot be preachers. I can't imagine listening to her preach. Awful. 0/10. Avoid her

Dr. Archibald gives way too much work for MECE 109 to be considered a two credit class. I often found myself struggling through the weekly homework and staying up super late to try to get it done before the deadlines. Also the amount of work required for the group project is astronomical.

Very difficult professor. His personal goal is to make all students lives miserable. Is a good lecturer and nice person but incredibly difficult. Tons of hw and group projects. Hw is very difficult. He will help if you can make it to office hours. Huge line for office hours all the time regularly waited an hour or more.

Good professor, just teaches challenges class. Had him for MECE 311, DFM and solid modeling and enjoyed all three classes, just had to put in a lot of work in order to do well. He is very helpful in office hours if you are unable to follow him along during lectures.

Dr. Archibald is a great teacher. He is very helpful in office hours. The class required a lot of work and the class exercises were very hard. The homework took up a lot of my time. However, I feel like I gained a lot of knowledge. He provides tutorials for Creo, which helps you to go back and understand how to model the parts if you need to.

Dr. Archibald is such an amazing guy. I'm not going to lie, but this class is tough but very beneficial. There are many projects/homework assignments and yes, group projects. He has been know to give some very low grades (and negative ones too) but as long as you follow the grading rubric you will be fine.  Go to office hours for help!
It was fine. Gen ed taken for credit.

Do not buy the book! Basically to pass the tests memorize your notes to the best of your ability. The tests are basically all essay questions. Seybold is a great guy and is very understanding and respectful.
Class is super interesting. Tests are hard but reasonable . As long you study, you're good!! Also he's super hot and a total gem so definitely worth it!! LOVE SEYBOLD

Dr. Seybold is an amazing professor. Take every opportunity to take a class with him. You definitely have to work to get a good grade, but he provides excellent support to students if you ask. He is extremely available to you via office hours and email.

Incredibly Intelligent and wonderful professor. Many find him to be hard, however the most of them do not work hard enough to learn the material. He is very welcoming to those coming into his office even outside of office hours. If you do your work, and are a responsible student you're going to do well.

One of the worst professors I have had. Spent most of the time talking about how successful he was. During the rare times he actually 'taught' he went over such a variety of topics it was impossible to know everything. He has explicitly decreed that no accommodations can be made for extenuating circumstances during online learning. Save yourself

He has a nice personality. A knowledgeable man, but not such a good teacher. His topics in class seem random and you aren't sure what the class is even about. The workload isn't super bad, but he is a BRUTAL grader. No partial credit or curves whatsoever. For papers his rubric is almost impossible to meet. 156 is manageable but very frustrating.

Spends a noticable amount of class time bragging about his past experience in industry and material assets, routinely behaves like students aren't as important as him, his grading criteria is almost always impossible to meet. I've had easily 3 times as many essay assignments in this class compared to my actual writing class.

Dr Russin is very tough. She moved very quickly through the material and took very little time to let the class catch up. Very lecture heavy--would be a lot better if she let us do group work or helped us out more with interactive assignments.

She is personable, easy to talk to, and even signed me into my second class with her. The students love her because she makes accounting interesting through hilarious stories of her experiences in the field. Each class was memorable and I looked forward to attending. It's a shame she's leaving the college, she was one of our best professors.

Professor Russin is a wonder teacher. I had her for Accounting 201 and learned a lot. Tests were very straight forward and she literally told us what was going to be in the test. Test grading is pretty much all partial credit. Great Professor. I would definitely take her again.

I had her for ACCT 201 and I love her. She shares the best stories from her time in the industry. She is very clear about what will be on the test. She makes the class easy to understand. I hope I can take more classes with her.
This is my second semester with Professor Russin and she continues her greatness. Would take her again and recommend her to anyone.
Test are impossible, rambles during lectures, get ready to fail unless you try very very hard.

Interesting soul, but tends to ramble about his own personal interests. Reading quizzes are easy as long as you actually do the reading. Tests are also easy, but ask him what he plans to test over--with our class, he gave us a final that included material he put on the network but that he never actually lectured on. Lenient grader on the paper.
Easy grader on papers and easy final exam. Lots of reading but survived without reading it all. Called Christ and the Media but focuses more of media ecology theories.

Dr. Gordon is the dearest, kindest, most well-read man that I know. He cares about all of his students and everything he encounters. He seems to know everything and yet always wants to learn more. I recommend him for any class you can get. I find it remarkable that, though he never assigns huge amounts of work, I always learn the most in his class.

He can be VERY condescending. He thinks he is far superior in every way to any of his students and does not hide such thoughts. Papers for his class bring your grade down and you definitely have to read to pass the quizzes. His tests are okay. He is not very helpful in office hours and usually talks over you the whole time.

Take Chisholm for Civ Arts if at all possible. She's kind, clear in her teaching, and makes it incredibly easy to get a good grade in her class as long as you show up for lectures and do a couple of hours of studying before each test. Civ Arts can either be a relaxing, easy A with Chisholm, or a complete time-suck with Munson. It's up to you.

She's an ideal HUMA professor. You can learn if you want because she knows a lot, but it is not hard to do well. You get what you want out of her class, but you're almost guaranteed an A if you try at all.

I thought I was going to hate the class. It ended up being one of my favorite classes that I've taken here (as a senior). Arts isn't my thing at all, but she actually managed to make it interesting. Tests were all easy A's, as was the paper. Definitely try to get her for 301.
Easy A. She is boring but it is easy to get her on a tangent to make class fun. You don't really need to show up to class. You can wiki the information and still get A's on the tests.
Exams are very fair, and study guides are given out before every exam with all of the information you will need to know. She is so kind and understanding with her students.

President McNulty conveys his ideas in a clear and simple manner. Understanding the U.S. Constitution is a broadly beneficial class enriched by McNulty's experience as an attorney at the Department of Justice. He is kind, funny, and knowledgeable. The exams are rigorous (multiple choice and true/false) but fair. Definitely worth taking!

Professor Dean is a challenging professor. He knows his stuff, but he often overcomplicates the material; a class of science and engineering majors needs less theory and more applications. Dean grades even correct answers extraordinarily harshly. When proved wrong after accusing a student of cheating, he refused to give any points back.

The quizzes are easy if you study the practice quizzes. Exams not so much. There are always curveballs and he's very picky with grading. Doesn't explain concepts well, thinks things are obvious. Prefers text instead of email, which is kinda uncomfortable. Very frustrating course, I switched my major progression to avoid taking him again.

Professor Dean, overall is a really good professor and teacher. Admittedly, he is a tough grader but as long as you use your book and the practice quizzes and tests he gives you effectively you will succeed.
A class for non-math majors KNOWINGLY taught at a math major level.
Great class if you are a genius. Otherwise avoid at all cost. Tests and quizzes are impossible. He requires lots of memorization of useless unnecessarily long theorems.

I have taken Dr. Anderson for SOCI 378, SOCI 471, and SOCI 452. I cannot say enough good things about him. He is the most precious old man. Most of his tests are take home, and you really don't need to do any of the HW reading assignments. He always makes himself available to you: office hours, quick email responses, etc. Easy A and an amazing man

Really nice guy, funny, and makes the class interesting. Lectures sometimes confusing or go too fast to properly take notes. Test questions can require specific names to be known that are only mentioned once orally. Ethnography project is tough, and exams need to be studied well for, but grades very fairly. Calls students out mid class.

Dr. Genaro is a gem. She is an excellent teacher, and definitely goes out of her way to make sure her students understand the material. There is a lot of homework outside of class, and how much you learn depends on the effort you put into it. She has review sessions prior to exams which are very helpful. Definitely don't miss bad behavior day!

She is really such an amazing professor and person. The class was lecture heavy but she taught it very well. The content was interesting and the tests were sort of tough. Papers were due fairly regularly but she always gave good feedback and time to revise them. I can't say enough good things about her, she is so genuine and a really great teacher.
Dr. Genareo's passion for education radiates through every thoughtful word she speaks in class. By far my favorite professor/class in college.

Dr. Genareo is kind and works hard to make sure her lectures are engaging and easy to understand. There are plenty of writing projects and the observation is certainly not one to procrastinate but overall, this class is well worth it even for non-ed majors. Dr. Genareo's only flaw is her occasional tendency to rush for the sake of staying on track.

She is such an amazing woman and educator. She truly cares about each individual student and will work with them with whatever they need. Classes can get long depending on the topic. I never dreaded going to a Genareo class

Dr. Snyder is a great guy. Took him for 2 classes. Has a lot of real world knowledge that he utilizes to effectively teach classes and relation to the real world. His current events are also really good. Always willing to help. Hes not that old, probably late 60s, but he acts as though he is much older, making fun of his age etc. Can be very funny.

I had him for his international studies course and he was extremely knowledgeable on the subject. He has a plethora of work experience traveling to foreign countries for business activities and he leverages them to engage his students. His current events help increase awareness for global business and instills a sense of passion in his students.

Professor Hogsette is funny and engaging, but the entire semester was built around transhumanism. Every article we read, paper we wrote and movie we watched had to do with transhumanism, and it got pretty repetitive pretty fast. While he certainly loves the topic, it seemed a lot to ask us all to be as enthusiastic as he was.

Loved his lectures, loved his crazy movie assignments. He had a terrible final study guide, and there was no way to know what to study for the exams. In class writings were reasonable but the testing was a crapshoot and I spent 4 hours studying for like 3 days. He had us read Dante's Inferno and asked us about tiny minutia, not said in class.

Taking Civ Lit as an English major meant that the class was a breeze, but Hogsette is so much fun to take I knew I wanted to take it with him. He clearly didn't care as much about this course as other majors courses, but I still enjoyed taking it with him. He assigns lots of movies to watch, and there was no midterm!
Dr. Hogsette is a lot of fun in class, and I really enjoy taking classes from him.

Dr. A gave lots of individualized attention and was easily accessible outside of class. I had a lot of questions and insecurities in the class, but he guided me through very well. VERY easy grader if you actually try a little. Lectures can be a tad dull, but that's kinda on the subject. He is such a gem and I'm excited to take more classes with him

Dr. Anderson is a very smart man. He knows that he has non-sociology people in his introductory class so he explains things really well. Class is all lecture based so it can get very boring and long. But overall, he cares about his students, both academically and personally.

Not really a hard class, but it is ridiculously boring. All exams (including the final) are open book and notes, so it's advisable to take thorough notes in class. The exams aren't that bad, but can be a little hard to decipher, so you still need to study beforehand. Papers and the group project are pretty manageable, and he's not a harsh grader.

Dr. Anderson is a true gem! He is interesting, caring, and encouraging. His lectures are not as well organized and easy to follow as they could be; expect to read a lot if you hope to gain mastery of the material. All exams are OPEN NOTE, however! Dr. Anderson genuinely enjoys his students and the subject he teaches. He is amiable and friendly. <3

Dr. Anderson is one of the most caring professors I have ever had. He is sincerely interested in the material. Although the lectures can seem a bit long at times, the material is interesting. Also - he lets students use notes on exams. Shows real interest in those taking the class.

Dr. Ligo cares about her students fiercely! She'll want to help you in any way she can, thus she can give out a lot of homework compared to other courses. All the papers we turned in were in Spanish, so be prepared to make use of what is learned in class. Taking notes isn't too hard if you focus on her begininng list of "this is what we'll cover."

Hotel? Trivago Language Requirement? Ligo Pro Tip: Use the whole test time, then tell her you're struggling and she'll just have you finish it whenever you want in her office.  more study time and she helps you get stuff right.

She cares about how much you are learning and not only your grade. She is willing to sit down one on one and not make you feel bad or dumb for not understanding something. Great professor, great person.

She is such a wonderful lady. She is so passionate about Spanish and her lectures are so interesting and engaging. She really understands that students struggle and cares about them. I went in with 4 years of Spanish and was fine. She mostly speaks in Spanish in class. She brought us chocolate from Mexico and made us tres leches cake. fun class

She understands that you may not be there because you want to be. She gives you the tools so that you can succeed not only in the class, but also as you take upper level Spanish classes. She is fun and caring, and she'll also try to make Spanish as fun as possible. There are a lot of homework assignments due each class, but that's just Spanish.

His classes have been consistently the worst that I have ever taken. He is disrespectful, insensitive, doesn't seem to be aware of what the content he is supposed to be teaching is, and often just doesn't come to class. He made me want to drop out of college.

I took Comm 207 last semester and had a blast. It's what I call an honest class - it's hard, but if you put in the time, you will succeed. Classes were interesting and he brought pizza to our 7pm Saturday final. :) The difficulty lies more with the subject itself because there is so much to remember, but Dr. Brown makes it a fun experience.

I love all of my communication classes and tend to do well in all of them except this one. I was not fond of how he ran the class. He would assign hw and expect us to complete it and when people didn't do the hw he would make an exception for them for full credit, and not reward those who were responsible enough to do the hw with extra credit.

Dr Brown is s a great guy, but his class is tough. It takes effort to get a good grade. There's a ton of reading and the textbook is hard to understand. She is so nice. She is always willing to help students. I learned a lot from her! and I would like to take more her classes.
She is really helpful and caring . Whenever i needed help she was really helpful and willing to help students. i really enjoyed her class !

Loved Mrs. Anderson. Such a sweet lady, so incredibly kind-hearted and helpful. She has a true passion for the material. Definitely read for the quizzes, but you will do just fine in the class if you put a little effort into it. :)
She was great. Not very difficult. One of the nicest profs at GCC.

SUPER EASY. Nice enough guy. I hear the course is getting revamped for incoming freshmen and beyond, but I imagine it will still be crazy easy. If you pay attention in class (no outside studying) you are almost guaranteed an A.
very easy, test are a joke, homework is an automatic 100%, and gives a ton of extra credit, take him if you can!!!

He is good.
He's super boring, but a really nice guy. The tests are exactly like the practice tests, so as long as you can figure out the practice tests, you'll have no problem. Don't get the textbook.
He is nice, really he is but honestly instead of paying attention in class read the power points its the only way you will pass.

I loved taking Dr. Leon's classes! She is a hoot and has done some pretty amazing things in her life.... and she was born in Paris, need I say more? Dr. Leon is superb, she is fair and always lends a helping hand to those that ask. I had the pleasure of taking 4 of her courses and was her "petit esclave" (TA). Dr. Leon is the most hilarious person I've ever met and very, very knowledgeable.  She may offend the Grover types, but that's usually a good thing.  Her class is a fair amount of work and a whole lot of fun.

Dr. Houk is so so great. She's very clear in her lectures and has a lot of past experience in the field that makes her very relatable.  Definitely role model material.

Dr. Birmingham is brilliant, and though this was only an introductory engineering class, it showed. She was very helpful and clear as well, and made the class as difficult as the level it was: introductory.

The best professor Grove City has to offer. The only argument you could make to that is Dr. Allison.

She is actually is at SRU.  She's a very good teacher but she leaves a lot of the reading and work up to you. Best teacher ever

By far, Dr.Donnelly was the best educator I ever had.  He gave me an appreciation of the arts that I can never repay.  The skills he taught have proven invaluable. she is awesome! really makes sure you know everything and explains it multiple times before a test

Awesome prof - they should hire her full time to teach principles of accounting.I took her class in '09 and I still remember it all because she made it fun to learn. Also - she is pretty good looking ;)

a great, helpful, nice teacher. an easy class, but at times boring. he lets students get distracted and does not engage them super well. a light workload. just be more appreciative of what he teaches and u'll learn things u could implement in ur future. pay attention to details, and it will earn u an  A.

I hated class w/ Gonzalez. I could never understand what was going on & when I asked how I could do better, he told me to go to another professor for help. His tests are ridiculously hard & have nothing to do w/ what was taught in lecture. He's a brilliant man and pretty nice outside the classroom, but he can't teach. He should stick to research.

I completely understand why Iowa State did not keep his tenure. If I was Iowa State I would have fired him a long time ago. Expect for him to treat you like a 9th grader, then he will fail the class. No help whatsoever. Hopefully GCC feels the same way soon.... AVOID THIS CLASS, AVOID THIS TEACHER! He is a research prof. not a teacher.
Don't expect to be spoonfed. His lectures can often be organized to make them a little confusing, but he is also great one-on-one. Tests are also difficult, but very fair.

Basically, Trammell is awesome. He is good at his job and knowledgeable in his field, helpful when you need it, and often quite fun to listen to. The only legitimate complaint I've ever heard against him pertains to the difficulty of his tests. And they are difficult, but not unreasonably so - and he always adds a nice curve to soften the blow :)

Dr. Trammell is great- kind, funny, interesting, a little eccentric, and smart. He is exactly what I thought a professor would be like prior to coming to college! :) One of my favorite moments was when he brought in an elephant ear leaf to show us. (The tests are a little tough, but if you put in the reading/studying time, you can do very well.)

Give this man a hug.  Dr. Trammell deserves an award for his unbelievably kind and gracious attitude.  Symbolic Logic is a rough course, but Dr. Trammell makes you want to take the course again.  Engages the class with fantastic phrases from a simpler time such as "willie nillie" and "ditsy-doodlin'" around.  Office is classic.

Symbolic Logic is a difficult course, but Dr. Trammell is an amazing prof. He always makes himself available for help with homework and understanding concepts. His tests are extremely mind boggling, but he generously curves, to make it almost impossible to fail. Kind, helpful, and funny, he embodies the best of college profs.

Got my philosophy minor w/ only Trammell classes. General and Symbolic Logic, and Biomedical Ethics were my favorites. His tests are the hardest at GCC, but he curves to make up for it. Knows his stuff and keeps you engaged. Offers tutoring on Mon. & Wed. Is a very kind man in class and outside of class. His wife is great too!

I usually felt like I had spent more time preparing for class than Melinda had. She is very busy, and teaching here is clearly very low on her priority list. Changes syllabus without any explanation, gives arbitrary work, assigns reading that doesn't relate, and then stands at the front of the room skimming the textbook for things to talk about.

Melinda focuses the majority of the class on philosophy (e.g. Plato, Aristotle...)  Often changes the syllabus without actually changing the syllabus, then gets angry when her students are confused. Anxiously waiting for your paper to get handed back? Forget about it. Melinda is generally easy going ... I think she's just spread too thin.
Excellent class.  Tests are not too hard.  Class is enjoyable.
Dr. Adels challenges you in this class, but she is always willing to help.

If you pay attention in class, read the chapters on your own time, & occasionally go to office hours you will get a solid grade in the class. She is a fair grader. Projects are easy points (20-40% of total grade depending on which class) - put time into it and USE COLOR for turned in projects and you will get an A. #1 tip: GO TO HER OFFICE HOURS!

Had her for buslaw. Told us what to study for tests and if you studied practice problems you were fine. Not to difficult. She was interesting and fair with grading. I didn't do much work at all and didn't need to study much and was fine. Overall for buslaw i highly recommend
Pay attention to her in class examples.
Cool dude, lots of humorous anecdotes in class. If you make sure to know book definitions for his tests and take decent notes, you will be fine...

Dr. Sparks is a lot of fun and he makes the material click.  He practiced law for a number of years, so he is generally able to incorporate examples from the real world into his lectures, which is cool.

Dr. Sparks the man.  He has a dry sense of humor, but is hilarious at the same time.  He will answer any question you have about the material, and loves to help his students do well in his class.  He uses the Socratic method in the class, which really makes the discussion of the cases worth coming to class.  Great class!
What?  A self-impressed individual.  Please spend more time preparing for class.  The law is not static.

Dr. John A. Sparks is Dean of the Alva J. Calderwood School of Arts and Letters at Grove City College and teaches U.S. Constitutional History and Business Law. Sparks is a Fellow for Educational Policy with the Center for Vision & Values at Grove City College. He is a graduate of Grove City College (BA Economics) and the University of Michigan Law

Mrs. Seguin is awesome - what a prof should be.  She treats her students like adults. Instead of dictating everything down to the letter she provides simple outlines to assignments that allow creativity and passion. I also appreciated that she does not have strict deadlines - just do the work and you're good.

Absolutely clueless about the material.Don't bother asking her for help on your project because she won't know the answer and never took the time to learn the software or the cameras or any other equipment. Easy A because she just blames the technology when your project is missing.

Great at teaching the concepts of C++ and communicating with his students. However, a few programs in this class were particularly brutal; fortunately he's very approachable during office hours and is very willing to help you work through your program. He docks a lot of points for seemingly small errors on your programs, but isn't overtly unfair.

Gribble is neither the most clear nor the most confusing teacher. He has high expectations, which would be fine, BUT the man has no concept of partial credit. His rubrics are weird and will penalize you by huge amounts for trivial errors. His test questions often require more than is actually asked for to get full credit. Expect GPA to drop.

In GCC's computer program, you seldom see revies on the profs since you seldom have any choice about what prof you have. Gribble is hard but pretty good, though he freaks out regularly about computer use in his class.

He's  a great prof. he's clear and and reasonable, he expects you to work hard and to take the time to understand the material, but will try his best to make sure you succeed. His programs are hard and some people may complain that he is a little bit to hard, but chances are may of them should not be taking the course in the first place.
Mrs. Miller is so amazing.  She really knows what she is talking about.  She is funny and gets to know everyone.  I am learning a tremendous amount from her.
Very boring in class, teaches straight from a power point which is straight from the book, not required for anything. Pretty nice guy, but not worth the time.
He was approachable but definitly boring in class with quite a bit of notes.
Very easy class.  Not the most interesting, but he does the best with the material given.  Very easy tests - only about 15-20 minutes each.
Lou was a great teacher. The school, his students, and undoubtedly me will miss him greatly.
This guy is an old school prof... he's a tough guy but you end up loving the guy...
This guy is friggin awesome.  I only have him once a week, but he is definately the best sax teacher i have ever had.

Fantastic organ professor.  Come to Grove City to study with Dr. Konzen and learn on the Kimball and Noack instruments.  Recent graduates have gone on to Yale, Indiana, and Notre Dame for more organ study.  The opportunities, resources, and attention at GCC for organ students are unparalleled.

My favorite memory of Konzen was when he got mad at students for arriving to a morning class 5 minutes late even though he usually arrived 15 minutes late.  He proceeded to scream at all of us and then threw a bunch of records at the class.  I learned nothing in the class except to stay away from him.  He is also a terrible orch director.

Goes off on random tangents a lot, talks about food a lot, likes to hide actual information somewhere with these random things.  Needs to let EVERYONE know about upcoming tests and hw assignments, not just 2 people.
Total Jerk!!!!!!!!!
This man could use a course in ethics and a good slap upside the head for the grief he causes students due to his pompous arrogance, favoritism, and unprofessional attitude.

A fantastic professor who genuinely cares for all of his students.  I had him the first semester he was at GCC and have only grown to respect him more since then.  Almost switched my major to history because i enjoyed his class that much. Lectures are engaging and relevant.  Courses he teaches are not super easy but will be well worth your time.
Dr. Wyneken is one of the best professors at Grove City, hands down.

by far the best prof ive had at grove city. lectures are fantastic and he does a great job of engaging students. makes attending class worth while and is a down to earth guy who loves nebraska football. not a prof that will screw athletes. tests are all essay but study and you will be fine. i definitely recommend him

Dr. Wyneken is a great prof.  I learned so much from him in my first class that I schedule a couple more with him.  A great lecturer, and makes the material interesting.  Not easy and the readings are important, but he picks great books and you'll learn a ton.  I'd recommend him to anybody.

Very challenging class, but you learn so much so it is very rewarding in that respect. He is very passionate about what he teaches, and very interesting. Midterm and Final are 100% essays, and quizzes are from textbook. You learn a ton from him. I actually left the class wanting to learn more. You have to work hard, but totally worth it!
Very hard to follow when conducting.

Dr. Arnold is very competent and experienced at music education administration. He's been in almost every administrative position you could think of, and he has tremendous wisdom and advice in all music ed-related topics. He doesn't run classes too rigorously, but he's always concerned with helping students understand and learn educational methods.
Showed favoritism. Very old fashioned and can be senile.  He is stubborn and tends to have temper.  GCC Music Department has gone down hill greatly.  Many holes in the curriculum. Stay away.

Doc is a tremendous administrator and business man.  He is most definitely the best head of the department this college will ever have.  Doc is very educationally oriented and people tend to forget this when they evaluate him.  He is not a great musician but that quite frankly is not his strength area.  He is amazing at what he does.

He's not a musician, he gets Eric Lassick to drive down to do band camp FOR him. He was late for my woodwinds methods class several times. I skipped 6 classes and he yelled at me but didn't lower my grade... he's usually very cranky. He's my advisor this semester but can't seem to answer even the simplest of my questions.
He's awesome and knows my name!
He is so awesome! I couldn't think of a better choir professor.

I've taken a number of courses (solfege, advanced and beginning conducting, choir) at Grove City with Dr. Browne, and he is very knowledgeable in all the areas that he teaches. He really tries to reach out to students and get them to perform their best. However, he does not accept messing around or tardiness. He's a fun loveable guy.

I have him for Choir. He's great. Though if you can take being told you're wrong, don't join choir. He says himself that he rarely compliments people. He often picks on the sopranos and altos, but only when they really are wrong. Overall he's great and is tough enough to make you better.

Incredibly intelligent.  Endless supply of knowledge that every musician should try to take advantage of before graduating.  He can be very elitist at times though but he does not do it to be mean or spiteful, it is simply his personality.  One of the best profs at Grove City without a doubt.
The man. Nothing else need be said.

Worthless.  If you're not studying the language outside of class and working with material outside of the book in addition to prescribed homework you'll probably be questioning what he's saying in class.  He likes to use German words that you haven't used before and the test does not reflect what's "taught" in class.
Great teacher! Funny stories.

Great man.  Always love how he opens class just saying whatever the heck is on his mind.  Good old school teacher.  Spend a little time each day studying and you'll ace the class.  Just have fun in the class, I did.
I love Herr Cole. He was a great professor and now that I've graduated I really miss being able to take classes with him! He's such a nice elderly gentleman.
Allgaier is not always a bad teacher, but sometimes he is. One moment you'll know what's going on, the next he's lost you and he won't care. Tests are unpredictable and tough. Study a lot.

His lectures are very hard to follow, his tests are unpredictable, and he doesn't tend to give good explanations when students ask questions. Don't take this prof. if you want to be able to do calculus, or get a good grade.
Take him if you want to pay a professor to read you the book.  He writes the theorems and then proofs for everything from the book.  Very little explaining of concepts.  Avoid at all costs!

As a person, he is great. As a teacher, he has his draw-backs. He is pretty poor at explaining new concepts to students?even in his office hours. I?m a quick learner when it comes to math, and I paid attention in class, but I still got very little out of his lectures. His tests are unpredictable and vary substantially in difficulty.
Nice man, great teacher. very diffucult tests though.

Tough subject matter, lectures fast, taking notes and reading is a must if you want to do well. But he is very helpful and always willing to help you out. Really cares about his students and takes intrest in seeing them grow into good entreprenuers.

Is an amazingly devoted and passionate man and strong Christian. Wants students to succeed, and will see to it that you do succeed, and take away something from the class. Great professor. He invests much time and preparation into classes, and makes everything relevant, interesting, and understandable! He is a very accomplished and inspiring man.

Passionate about entrepreneurship, reaches out to students to help them understand not only the material in class, but how to apply it in real-life situations. Definitely an excellent teacher. Highly recommend
My favorite math teacher so far. Not too hard, really tries to help students understand material.

Slightly above average discrete math professor. She gives out a note outline that is well written and expounds upon it during class, making it very easy to take comprehensive notes which then makes the homework easy. Not the most interesting prof I've ever had but definitely not the worst: see Jackson, Michael.
Very easy to understand.  Not the most interesting, but I never had trouble in her class.
No Comments
I had her for calculus one and she was fantastic. She teaches you a lot without making the class too difficult. Recommended.
Great Professor. Very down to earth, cares greatly about the students. Makes class enjoyable, yet still learn a ton. Strongly recommend
Definitely one of, if not the best professors on this campus.  She is by far my favorite.  She actually cares about my life and how what she's teaching applies to it.

I think Dr. Scott is the nicest and most open-minded prof at GCC. She is a caring woman and truly a joy to  be around. She loves controversial viewpoints and encourages class participation. I would stay at GCC if all of my profs were as down to earth and awesome as she is.
Best teacher that ive had at grove city, not only does she make concepts easy to understand she also is an understanding person. not too hard on the eyes either.
Fantastic
Dr. Dent is the best prof I've ever had, she goes out of her way to help her students understand tough concepts. Study hard and you'll be fine.

Go to her office hours, go to her office hours, go to her office hours.  She is an extremely hard grader and if your grades are important to you you will likely be very discouraged, but she is more helpful in office hours than any other professor I've had.  It will make a world of difference for your grade and your enjoyment of the lab.
hard!
hands down hardest prof here.  Her genetics class is the hardest class offered.  With that said I'm still taking her for genetics and developmental because she is that good and demands perfection
She's a very nice person, but a crazy hard teacher! I hope you took AP bio in high school. I feel very sorry for anyone who has to take her for baby bio.

I never took this prof but my mom did years and years ago. She still brags about how great a man he was. both in and out of the classroom. I have had dinner with him many times and love the man dearly. He no longer teaches but he is still on campus quite often. If you get a chance to meet him take that chance and especially if you are an accountant

Dr. Flemming is the worst prof at GCC. His tests are unfair.  His lectures, while boring, have very little to do with the tested material. I disliked him so much that i switched from an accounting major to finance. All of the positive options on him are just the GCC (oh he makes you work but he is "good" mindset) TAKE ANYONE OVER FLEMMING!!!!!!!!!!
No Comments
I took him over intersession and it was not bad at all. fleming was very personable and helpful, he assigns a heavey workload but i feel like a learned the material thoroughly
he's the only prof that teaches intermed... so if you are going to take intermed, take his principles as well
He can be very slow, but he knows his accounting.  He's incredibly nice, and he started handing out worksheets for test practice that are very helpful.

Dr. Patterson is a very nice guy. His teaching style may not be the clearest or most efficient, but he will answer any question you have.  The pace is somewhat slow, but in accounting this can be beneficial.

He's a genuinely nice guy. Seems to me like he's fed up with arrogant students who think they're smarter than him. He relies on and expects you to read the book for class. Not too much work other than that and easy online homework occasionally.

If you're looking to understand accounting, Mr. P's probably not the prof for you--unless you're willing to teach yourself a bit of the material. Trust me, you'll save yourself a lot of frustration if you take another prof. His requirements may be "easy" (debatable, IMO, since acct's learned by practice), but you'll pay in spades with his lectures.

I hate him. He is stupid and pathetic. He doesn't teach you anything, and when you ask him questions, he looks at you like you are an ape. His brain is the size of a piece of chalk. I had to teach myself EVERYTHING from the book. You won't learn how to do accounting at all. Basically it is a vocab lesson - one you want to sleep through.
She's a great teacher, enthusiastic and very helpful.  As a native Japanese, she has experienced the difficulty of learning another language.  I think maybe I love her.

Toba-sensei is super nice and super awesome. The more you get to know her the more you love her. She's really interested and concerned about every student and wants everyone to do well. If you don't understand something just ask and she will take the time to answer every question you have in detail. ALSO SHE'S AMAZING AND I LOVE HER.
No Comments

Well, I enjoyed the class because I wanted to learn Japanese already. She was a good teacher and was a good source of information about Japanese culture and language that wasn't in the book. You'll have to participate in class (respond vocally) and there'll be times when you don't know what she wants but...it's no biggie, it'll happen to everyone.

Had her for baby bio lab and I did not like it.  Very scattered.  Had us doing several different things during one lab.  Had difficulty filling out the lab worksheets and would make up some stuff and still get perfect scores.  Got a good grade, but did not enjoy the lab

this lady is totally weaksauce. speaks inaudibly in a thick southern drawl. she's more boring than any single person i've ever encountered. coupled with the temperature in rockwell, do yourself a favor and take baby chem or baby physics. eff this.
AHHHH.

My worst nightmare. Just call me "C girl." Studying or not, I bomb everything in this awful class. I can't hear her and I fall asleep. She's awfully nice but mostly awfully hard. I would go as far as calling her a "Textbook Floozie" because everything she uses comes straight from the book.
A very nice lady, but she's really boring and unorganized.  She doesn't explain things very well.  Study for the test ALOT.  It's alot harder than Shaws

As a French major, I've had quite a few classes with her.She's also my advisor. She's such a sweetheart! A little redundant at times (stories), but always willing to help. Her homework isn't too hard, and her tests are passable. But you MUST do the work to keep up. Slacking isn't advised.(and she gives you chocolate if you go to office hours!=D)

Trammell is a total babe.  Have you seen her red suit/crocodile skin shoes combo?  Phonetics helped me understand what others were saying to me a little better.  The class period is next to worthless but attendance is graded.

One of the quirkiest but most fun professors out there. She's a blast, but does make you work for your grade - not an insane amount of work, but work nonetheless. She's always willing to clarify or help in any way she can! She's great!
Madame is my favorite professor ever! I just love how fun and quirky she is.  Her homework and tests are not hard if you study a little. Definitely take her for French classes.
She is awesome. Very helpful, a good deal of homework but it really helps you learn the language.
Douche.  Bag.

Took classes with him for two years. Intelligent man. Pushed me to be better every day. He plays favorites; however, his "favorites" are everyone who works their hardest. Does NOT like slackers. Did blame my faith for me not doing well. Was slightly offended, but it motivated me to do better (and my faith benefited too). Occ. a jerk...but it works.

Still there, but for how long is anyones guess.  Guy is a total ego-maniac.  Teaching has taken a huge dropoff.  Tries to turn anything in your problem about your relationship with God.  Wants to put you in position to make you totally helpless and reliant on him.  Will never know real grade.  Had him for A&P 1 and 2 as well as pathophysiology.

Dr. Weber is an amazing man.  He is very encouraging and wants his students to do well.  However, even more than that, he is concerned about their relationships with God.  This class is a ton of work and yeah Dr. Weber does make it hard, but you will learn a lot!

What would I have done without taking his class?  I am currently at Harvard Med and at the top of my class. I credit Dr. Weber with my success.  While his class was crazy hard, and I hated it at the time, don't miss out.

My favorite professor, hands down. He takes  note of those who are there to learn and those who are there for the grade. If he likes you, he'll go out of his way to help you succeed in college and out of college. Informative lectures punctuated by hilarious personal stories. Tilford keeps history fun and relevant.
Very smart man! Knows everything about history and Alabama football. Sad to see him go:(
AWFUL ... thank goodness he's leaving

I'm a biology major, but I loved taking his classes better than my own major classes! He is hard but that's because he WANTS you to understand thoroughly.  He's funny as all get out too.  Ask him about Oog and Moog or Southern Culture.

Tilford is an amazing professor, very funny and well worth attending class for. Your hand will hurt at the end of each class from the speed at which you must take notes but it's well worth it. (Do not take if you absolutely hate history, foreign policy or the military)

Good teacher and REALLY NICE person!  She will go out of her way to help any student... BUT, you have to take the initiative and seek her help by asking questions, demonstrating your interest. Take her class, by all means. She makes the class interesting with lots of real-life examples from Japanese culture and everyday life.

She throws in a lot of interesting information about Japan and the language beyond what we learn in the textbook. Sometimes she says awkward things in English accidentally that are absolutely hilarious. Very nice prof.

He's a great professor because he truly just wants you to know the material and learn something. He is usually in the financial office but teaches this class. Come to class before tests. He lets you know what to study before the tests so you're not surprised by the information. Not terribly interesting lectures, but not bad. I would take again.
